comments: Fantastic welcome from Carol with many goodies left for us including coffee wine and biscuits. Cottage itself was beautiful with many original features - with special mention to the roll top bath and the stoop. We found everything we needed and appreciated the stove top kettle during load shedding! Loved the outside (hot) shower and visits from the charming cat. Only minor downside, the proximity of the trains enthusiastically tooting in the mornings from 6am. Overall a truly delightful stay.
name: Pineapple Cottage - Kalk Bay
description: The cottage is an old style Victoria cottage with wooden floors, sash windows and shutters. The floors are painted white, ceilings white and walls a light color to give it a open light look. There is lots of light and sunshine.
neighborhood_overview: We are situated close to the fishing village, a heritage site of many generations of fishermen and families. The community is friendly. The cottage is situated a short minute or two walk away from the Kalk Bays main road shops and restaurants including the harbour. One can enjoy a short stroll to the local beaches and tidal pools. In fact guests don't even need to leave Kalk Bay, there is loads to enjoy, just a short distance away from the cottage.
host_about: I live in Kalk Bay, Cape Town  with my husband, Tom, and our 2 sons. We love living in Kalk Bay. It’s a suburb of Cape Town but yet a village in itself. We get to enjoy the ocean, the restaurants, the shops, the people plus all other unique characteristics of Kalk Bay … right on our doorstep!

name: 8 On Burnside Under Table Mountain!
description: This trendy garden apartment with large bedroom and bathroom en suite and kitchen leads to a sunny courtyard & is situated exactly where you want to be when in Cape Town! Free Wi Fi available. We also have a solar powered inverter so therefore have uninterrupted electricity. Ask about laundry service- Close to everything. Expert advice& tips! Superb Location with loads of trendy bars and restaurants close by  and easily accessible on foot  but not so close as to be noisy.
neighborhood_overview: Our neighbourhood is a lovely quaint and interesting area. It has beautiful Victorian houses and quiet streets and its inhabitants are creative people of all ages. We have some amazing markets such as the Biscuit Mill in Woodstock and and the famous neighbourhood goods market the Oranjezicht market situated in the Waterfront . We have lovely parks, one of which De Waal park hosts free summer concerts in summer on Sunday afternoons showcasing amazing artists (always South African)within walking distance. We are also on the clean and safe new miciti bus route which would take you to either the airport beach or to the city or in fact anywhere you may want to go including the waterfront. I love that you can walk to everything and that we are so close to trendy Kloof Street which has many bars restaurants and bistros. Many of the important tourist attractions in Cape Town such as Table Mountain and the Company's Garden as well as the most important and visited museums are within walking dis
host_about:
l was born in Cape Town and am passionate about my beautiful city and country, South Africa. I love nothing better than to introduce people to this wonderful City and that is why I am a Tourist Guide. Travelling and visiting new countries and experiencing the cultures thereof is my dream activity and I have therefore  travelled  widely through Europe, UK and have visited Egypt, Morocco, Thailand, Vietnam, Cambodia, Bali as well as Namibia, Mocambique, Comores Island, Mauritius and the USA. My hobbies are Travelling of course as well as Reading, Cinema and Theatre , Live Music, arts and culture as well as cycling, hiking, outdoor activities and gaining knowledge of other countries and cultures. I happily engage in discovering new and wonderful activities and restaurants, visiting the  winelands and exploring nature.
Hospitality and service are paramount as well as providing information to make your stay as comfortable and as enjoyable as possible. Registered Tourist Guide with public driving permit and operating licence  with registered vehicle so able to do tours when time allows.  My Motto: Your thoughts become your words and your words become your reality. Never stop dreaming! Positivity is everything! Love life!

name: Llandudno Penthouse  5 mins / beach / No loadsheds
description:
Penthouse - 5 Minutes from Llandudno beach.
Sleeps 2 adults.
Modern, bright, spacious, stand-alone penthouse with open plan, lounge, dining & kitchen area plus balcony with table/2 chairs & barbecue.
approx. 85 sq meters
Stunning view of Little Lion's Head.
There is one bedroom up fairly steep stairs in the loft.
TV/Netflix & free Wi-fi.

Solar powered/no load shedding.
Shops, restaurants & tourist attractions in nearby Hout Bay.
neighborhood_overview:
Llandudno is a very sought after upmarket area & is located on the Atlantic coastline.Llandudno is a 30 minute drive to Cape Town & is approx 5 minutes from nearby Hout Bay.
Cape Town International Airport is a 40 to 45 minute drive to Llandudno.
Llandudno has one of the most beautiful beaches in the world.
Sandy Bay is a nudist beach & is within easy walking distance (approx 20 minutes) from the car park with stunning scenery on route.

Chapman's Peak Drive / World of Birds / Hout Bay Harbour Market /

name: Central and Beautiful
description: Come and enjoy the beautiful and tranquil space with a deck overlooking the World Cup stadium and the V&A Waterfront. The apartment is comfortable and very central, it is walking distance from most things worth seeing in Cape Town: V&A Waterfront, CBD and the Cape Quarter, even the beaches are not far off.  Because we are against the mountain with a view over the harbour the property has a lot of steps.
neighborhood_overview: Greenpoint is a beautiful and relatively safe neighbourhood, it is also great for walking, especially to the W&A Waterfront or to the Cape Quarter with all its great restaurants and pubs.  The city centre is 5 minutes away and fun to explore on foot, including the Bo-Kaap with its bright coloured houses or the business of Long street.  There is also MyCiti bus stops all around and also right in front of my apartment as well. (Although the area is safe it is important to use an Uber or taxi after dark)
host_about:
I have a great sense of humor and love being with people although I am also a very private person, I love to cook and entertain people around good food and wine.

 I love adventure and feel passionate about travelling, if I have $1 left I will spend it on exploring a new destination.  I travel extensively and try to do at least 2 new destinations per year.  I like being on Airbnb because it gives me an opportunity to connect with others  that love travelling and that have been to places I still want to go OR come from countries I still want to see.

name: Cosy Downtown Loft with Views over Rooftop Deck
description: Our cozy little loft features an architect-designed interior consisting of wood, steel and exposed brick. During it's renovation in 2006 certain original features, including Oregon ceiling beams and parquet floors, where restored to help retain a sense of its Cape heritage. The industrial red brick wall and orange mosaic in the kitchen and bathroom reflects an element of quirk and youth.
neighborhood_overview: The loft is located in an old part of town, where art deco buildings and history is found around every corner. Saint George's Cathedral, The Company's Garden and old Slave Quarters, City Hall and the Central Library are all steps away.
host_about: I'm a tech enthusiast with a blend of design and coding expertise. Over two decades, I've lived across our vibrant town (Cape Town is in fact a small, little town) from the leafy suburbs to the charming seaside to the gritty inner city to the colourful city bowl. What began as assisting friends and family with their Airbnb listings has evolved into managing a diverse portfolio of properties, fostering opportunities for others in our community.

comments:
Wir waren einen Monat im Cottage von Elfi und Peter und müssen sagen, dass es das beste Airbnb-Erlebnis war, das wir je hatten!
Wir waren mit unserer 1 jährigen Tochter und einem befreundeten Ehepaar dort. Das Cottage bietet alles, was man für einen so langen Aufenthalt braucht, wie beschrieben. Insbesondere bei Alltagsgegenständen, die man u. a. mit einem Kind braucht, haben uns die Gastgeber zuvorkommend ausgeholfen und uns immer das Gefühl gegeben, willkommen zu sein.
Der Garten und der Pool sind super gepflegt, und die Möglichkeit, die Grillhütte und die große Terrasse mitzunutzen, hat uns sehr gefreut. Wir würden immer wieder das Cottage buchen. Ein Highlight war das gemeinsame Braai und die guten Gespräche, zu das die beiden uns eingeladen haben.

We were looking for a private space to get ready for a roadtrip and Peter's cottage had everything we could have hoped for, including a walled yard with a solid gate so we could pack our vehicle in peace and quiet, out of view of the street and neighbors.
The hosts were very friendly, advised us on what was available within walking distance. The cottage was clean and comfortable. They helped us with laundry and the use of their braai, since they live on the same property in the main house ... which is a real advantage and didn't affect our privacy one bit.
We will call again on our next trip to Cape Town.

name: Ocean Views | Open Plan | Pool | 4 Bed
description: Welcome to our vibrant Three-bedroom home in Green Point, where a bold yellow theme brings warmth and character to every space. Enjoy sweeping ocean views from the light-filled open-plan kitchen, dining, and living areas, with floor-to-ceiling windows and a balcony perfect for relaxing. A private pool on the ground floor offers a tranquil escape. Set in a quiet, secure neighbourhood with parking for one car, this home blends charm, comfort, and a touch of sunshine.
neighborhood_overview: Green Point is a popular leisure district dominated by its namesake park with lakes jogging trails a hedge maze and a biodiversity garden planted with indigenous flora. The park is also home to Cape Town Stadium a sleek sports and concert venue with tours. Espresso bars Italian restaurants and burger joints line Main Road and there are steakhouses and fish eateries along nearby Sea Point Promenade. The V&A Waterfront is 5 minutes away where you can shop till you drop or take a champagne cruise while watching the sun sink into the water on the horizon.
host_about:
I live between Cape Town, Africa, the Americas and Europe. Born German, I'm a true Global Citizen, love to travel and connect with new people wherever I go! If we meet you'll immediately my YELLOW-ness, as I've been wearing yellow every day for the last 5+ years.

For the last 10 years of travel, I staid at Airbnbs, making my host's houses my base to explore cities. I did also regularly share my beautiful Yellow House with Couchsurfing guests to connect with other cultures while staying home in Cape Town.

In 2021 I started renting out my beautiful Yellow House in Cape Town here on Airbnb and I'm happy to now share it with you for exclusive use while my nomadic journey around the world continues... Follow me on @YellowRandolf or rent my house to support my journey...

name: Cape Town Gardens Guest Suite
description: Your guest suite is large and spacious with a queen-size bed, a desk and tv, with fast-speed internet. There is loads of cupboard and drawer space and the large sash window looks onto the garden. There is a private en-suite shower room with generous towels and toiletries.
neighborhood_overview: In the Kloof Street Village neighbourhood. Accessible by foot into Cape Town Centre; also 5 minutes from beaches and The Waterfront.
host_about:
I used to own a Guesthouse,so have good experience in hospitality. Now I own a gorgeous Victorian Villa with sweeping views of Table Mountain. I have fully renovated the house, and it is intimate and cosy, but still with spacious Victorian-style rooms... oregon wood and wide sash windows.

My private self-catering apartment is in a perfect location to walk into the City or brouse the Gardens area, and it has a large outside patio looking onto Table Mountain

comments: We missed our flight and therefore arrived one day later, and it was no problem to check in before 15:00. It was me and my friend staying here for little over 1 week to both work and enjoy some free time. The apartment offers enough space to set up 2 workplaces in the living room and WiFi was fast and stable. There is a battery to power the WiFi and charge devices during load shedding, but during our stay luckily this wasn’t necessary. The apartment was light and spacious enough for us. We enjoyed the sunset from the living room (with windows that slide all the way open and has sun almost all day!) for as many nights as possible from the big chairs. Location was perfect for us as well and we felt very safe. Adele is a very sweet woman and it was also no problem to check out a bit later. Can highly recommend!

Nestled between the mountains and the ocean this sea facing apartment is centrally located and only a short walk from shops, cafes, restaurants and beaches.

A tranquil space to unwind and relax after a busy day whether it is work or leisure. Admire the crisp, contemporary decor of this bright and sunny living space and take in the peaceful surroundings and breathtaking sunsets.
neighborhood_overview: Sea Point is a beautiful coastal and upmarket suburb, boasting wonderful mountain and ocean views, as well as a diverse array of attractions.
host_about: I am Financial Manager working in Kuwait and have a place on airbnb as well (Cape Town). My husband is retired with two carehomes in the UK. I have two grown up children and one grandchild. We are keen sailors and currently building a home in the UK.

comments:
I stayed nearly two months in this cute appartment, which is in a safe and quiet location, within striking distance from restaurants, MyCiti bus stops, supermarkets and also within walking distance from the City. name: Spacious Heritage Cottage in the City-Safe&Cosy
description:
A super special little house in the absolute best part of town - a small little cobblestone cul-de-sac just off Kloof Street
THE APARTMENT IS FITTED WITH RECHARGEABLE lights and WiFi routers with a UPS to ensure CONTINUOUS WIFI SUPPLY for those wishing to conduct their BUSINESS/WORK UNINTERRUPTED.
central yet quite. Super safe and walking distance from everything. Perfect for design conscious travelers. Recently upgraded.
neighborhood_overview: The house is situated at the top of an old cobblestone cul-de-sac called King Street. King Street is very central, shooting off from popular Kloof Street which means it really is the best of both worlds. The house is mostly surrounded by an up-market hotel. So that means it's very safe (they have 24 hour security), and even though it's central it's very quiet. We can confidently say that it's one of the best locations to be in the city.
host_about:
We are an energetic couple living and enjoying the mother city. 
It gives us great pleasure to welcome visitors to Cape Town and assisting them in having an absolute great time. Jaco is an architect and Don an ex restauranteer, with 36 years experience in hospitality. During our leisure time we enjoy hikes, beaches, restaurants and spending time with family and friends.
Currently we are Co-Hosting. It is an absolute treat doing what we are doing.

name: Adenium Beachfront Mouille point
description:
You don’t get any closer to Cape Town’s Atlantic seaboard promenade and the ocean than the 2-bedroom Adenium Beachfront Apartment. Look out of the window to be met by palm trees, the sea, and maybe some dolphins that cruise this coastline. This is the Mouille Point Platinum Mile of very desirable residences and Adenium is right in the middle!
This property does not allow smoking at all.
neighborhood_overview: Adenium Apartment in Rhodora Court on Beach Road is one of the best positions for a holiday let apartment in Cape Town, along the Mouille Point Platinum Mile (very desirable residences). You can walk to V&A Waterfront, the Stadium, Greenpoint Park and numerous restaurants and cafés. The seafront promenade right in front of Adenium stretches for 3km, frequented every afternoon by walkers, joggers, bikers, skaters and dog walkers.
host_about: At Cape ES we offer a range of high quality, privately-owned holiday apartments in Cape Town that are brilliantly positioned and offer excellent value. The apartments range in size and location but all are positioned in close proximity to tourist sites, CBD businesses and Cape Town International Conference Centre.

comments: Patrick and Marina are wonderful hosts - very kind, proactive and superb communication. They proactively  reached out to inform us that a construction site had started next door after we booked and offered a discounted stay to accommodate. The noise was still very intense, which definitely impacted the stay, but this was not in their control. The place is beautifully decorated with all amenities in top quality. We had interpreted the photos and description a bit differently and thought the pool was adjacent to the room, whereas in reality it is next to the Manor House up a staircase and through their private garden. As they are super welcoming and kind that is not a problem though. Well located walking distance from Seapoint and Clifton beach. Thank you for hosting us!
name: Cottage de la Mer
description: Cook breakfast in a cosy kitchenette with stone surfaces and dine alfresco at a leafy bistro table on the private patio. Sleep like a baby in your King XL bed and enjoy the extra large saltwater pool and discover why this is the most desired location in Cape Town. Everything is within reach at this bright Cottage featuring floor-to-ceiling windows and natural colours.
neighborhood_overview: The Cottage is located in the affluent suburb of Fresnaye, a picturesque neighbourhood at the foot of Signal Hill where the rich and famous live. Walk down the palm fringed avenues to access trendy restaurants and food markets, then take a stroll along the beautiful Promenade. Within 5 to 10 minutes you can be at the popular V&A Waterfront, the famous Clifton Beaches, Table Mountain or Camps Bay.
host_about: I'm passionate about great design, luxury travel, top-notch restaurants, and fine wine. Originally from Germany, I now call breathtaking Cape Town - arguably the most beautiful city in the world - home. Together with my amazing wife Wenke (insta: @wenkeoehlandt), we’ll make sure you have an unforgettable time!

comments:
Great place local safe good parking .. central for just about everything in town . We were pre warned about the work and renovations that were ongoing .. weren’t really told about that it was still basically a building site on 2/3 floors above and below so a lot of noise during the day ..
The apartment was great for 2 persons everything was very clean well appointed .. had a glitch or two as this apartment had just been newly renovated after damage by smoke from a fire on floors above I think ?.. but Jenny was on it and things were sorted really quickly so hopefully no more issues .. 🙏👌❤️….
I would however if you’re a light sleeper the city is quite noisy in the evening up until midnight .. so use ear plugs ‘ 🫣🤣..after midnight it’s silent !
Lovely sun trap of a balcony in the afternoons and a comfy day bed with cushions nice touch.
My comments about the noise will eventually cease when they’ve completed the renovations so don’t be put off by that it’s worth it.
name: Fabulous 1-Bedroom De Waterkant Apt. WiFi included
description: This fabulous apartment has been newly transformed to take full advantage of the lovely light and views from the 5th floor.
neighborhood_overview: De Waterkant is the trendiest neighbourhood in Cape Town and one of the most exclusive. The apartment enjoys an enviable location in the heart of the city without actually being in the CBD (which virtually closes down on weekends). From the apartment, you can easily walk to most major attractions of Cape Town. The CBD is just a couple of blocks, The V&A Waterfront (shops, restaurants, bars, coffee shops and Robben Island) is only minutes away, and there's an excellent supermarket across the road. The CTICC (Convention Centre) is nearby. You can easily walk to the new Cape Town Stadium and Cape Quarter’s lively restaurant and café scene is just one block away.
host_about:
Hiya

I'm an Aussie girl from Sydney who'd been coming to South Africa for holidays for years and  thought she'd try and spend a little more time here. Didn't think that would turn out to be 14 years! 

 So I'm pottering around Southern Africa now, just hanging out with friends or grabbing my tent and going for 'a wander'.

I adore CT and love all the food/wine/beach/sunshine options

It's a great place to stay and is in an awesome location.

description: A peaceful, cool room in a family home in the leafy suburb of Kenilworth, Cape Town. We're friendly and warm hosts who love welcoming visitors to stay! name: SPOIL YOURSELF IN STYLE and COMFORT
description: Our private apartment offers an incredibly good feel vibe at 100sqm or 1076 feet 5 floor up,  offers decadent space in all rooms and is clean, neat well finished and incredibly well positioned yet off the busy street. Convenient, convenient, convenient. Close to the new and most trendy area for holiday makers in Cape Town.
neighborhood_overview: The apartment is situated in just off the retail hub in Sea Point opposite the new Upmarket Point Shopping Mall. The location pin shows the incorrect address some 250 meters further down the road which we have not been able to change due to a glitch on the system. host_about:
I'm born and bred Capetonian. I love to travel, enjoy meeting new faces and characters and love our city Cape Town. I enjoy the natural beauty our city has to offer, be it the beaches, oceans or mountains that surround us.

description: This modern two bedroomed apartment is on the 9th floor of a luxury apartment block called The Decks on vibey Long Street situated in the heart of the city. Free parking and free internet is available. A great location to explore fabulous Cape Town.
neighborhood_overview:
Long Street has many exciting pubs, nightclubs and restaurants serving a wide range of international cuisine to suit all tastes. I will gladly provide you with a list of great restaurants to visit near the apartment and in greater Cape Town. The famous Greenmarket Square (selling African curios and art) and the serene Company Gardens (housing the SA Museum & Planetarium) are around the corner from the apartment. Please see the photos for a visual feel of the area.
The apartment is ideally located with easy access to all the major tourist attractions including Table Mountain (5 mins by car), blue flag beaches like Clifton & Camps Bay (10 mins by car), the V&A waterfront (5 mins by car or 15 min walk), Company Gardens (5 mins walk) & Greenmarket Square (5 min walk).
You can take the MyCiti bus outside the apartment to the V&A waterfront area or Camps Bay & Clifton Beaches. The V&A is great for shopping and houses many restaurants and pubs. The two Oceans Aquarium is located i
host_about: I am a father,husband, entrepreneur & businessman. I am married to Cheryl for 33 years. Cheryl is an interior decorator  We have 3 adult children and 5 grandhildren and a German Shepherd dog. We enjoy traveling both locally &  overseas and love meeting  new people & e experiencing diffetent cultures.

comments: Isa is a warm and hospitable host. She welcomed us personally and went above and beyond to make sure we had all the comforts of her home. The house is incredibly beautiful, with exquisite art and lovely cockery! It is very conveniently located within easy walking distance to the beach, outdoor swimming pools, beautiful kitchenware stores like Blink, amazing supermarkets and restaurants. Try Kleinsky's for the most delicious hot filled bagels, Kanela coffee for brunch and outdoor seating, and Kimchi for Korean barbecue. Isa went out of her way during our trip to make sure we had everything we needed. I would definitely recommend staying here and wish we could have stayed for longer.
name: Roomy Art Deco flat, Fresnaye/Sea Point, Cape Town
description: Spacious art deco flat (117m2) with wooden floors and large rooms, 2 blocks from the sea front and Sea Point pool, walking distance to shops and restaurants. Great location, close to beaches & public  transport. 10 minutes to the centre of Cape Town & V&A Waterfront.
neighborhood_overview: I love the fact that I walk everywhere. I love the Sea Point pool which is a block away and the Promenade, a long stretch which runs along the ocean and where you can rent bikes. I love all the speciality food shops and also the local supermarkets - all within walking distance. You can't beat La Perla for a great view over the pool and a meal or drink outside - just a block away. Lots of good coffee shops on Regent Road, a stone's throw away. Mojo market has loads of different food options. Always easy to get Uber Eats or other Deliver services, like Mr Delivery and Order In. Get an Uber in a few minutes. Yoga and gyms within walking distance. I love the fact that Sea Point is relatively wind free.
host_about: I'm a Capetonian who loves Cape Town and I appreciate how lucky I am to live here. I love to travel out of the city and beyond South Africa's borders, within Africa and the rest of the world.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

comments:
A delightful, comfortable, spacious, light and airy, apartment with an excellent long balcony running the length of the living room and front bedroom.
Being high up we had wonderful views of Seapoint and the sea.
With a West facing balcony we enjoyed many great sunsets.
The recent installation of the inverter meant that the power outages didn't really worry us as we lost very little.
name: Terrace Apartment
description: Large apartment in Upper Sea Point. Two bedrooms, fitted with queen beds and newly renovated bathroom with rain shower. A lounge and separate dining area and a fully equipped kitchen with Induction hob, Nespresso Coffee Machine. The main bedroom and lounge open on to large terrace with great Atlantic Ocean views. Apple television,  high-speed wifi, air conditioning in main bedroom and an Inverter in the event of any electricity outages. There is secure parking in the complex.
neighborhood_overview: Great restaurants, a short drive to the Waterfront, City and Atlantic Beaches, walk down the hill to Sea Point main road precinct with shops and restaurants - hill a bit steep so may need a cab back!
host_about: Joint owner of a small boutique hotel in Johannesburg and one in Lagrasse in the South of France. Lover of food and wine. Live in Lagrasse and partly in Cape Town.

name: Merridew - Walking distance to Camps Bay Beach
description: Merridew is a spacious Camps Bay villa that is stylish and bright on the outside yet warm and inviting inside. Its six bedrooms easily sleeps 12 people so invite a large group of friends or family.
neighborhood_overview: Bordered by the spectacular Twelve Apostles mountain range and the glittering Atlantic Ocean, Camps Bay is one of Cape Town’s most breath-taking suburbs. As well as being a popular summer attraction for locals and tourists who flock to the beach, Camps Bay boasts restaurants and watering holes aplenty that line the famous sunset strip for fun that goes on long after dark.
host_about:
Turnkey365, is a division of the Capsol Group, which  began in 1996 as a tiny family-run business mainly to provide upmarket accommodation and associated services to the film industry in Cape Town at the time. 

Since then,we has developed alongside, and helped pioneer, the Luxury Villa Marketing and Rental Industry in Cape Town, particularly along the sought-after Atlantic Seaboard in areas such as Camps Bay, Clifton and Bantry Bay. Today we are a leader in Cape Town’s villa industry and is highly respected for our knowledge, professionalism and integrity. 

Having been around since the very beginning, we know this industry inside out; we know the best villas and apartments available to rent, we know how to market our properties in order to achieve the best possible returns and through our Property Management division we are able to provide peace of mind for our property owners. 

Most importantly we have learnt through experience what our local and international clients want and expect when renting, marketing or managing a villa in Cape Town. 

Over the years we have experienced significant growth, offering various specialised services via each of our sub-divisions. However, we still operates like a close-knit family that aims to provide you with the most comprehensive and professional service available.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

name: Self Cater in Rondebosch, Cape Town
description: Delightfully spacious three en-suite bedroomed centrally situated accommodation, upmarket & homely with fully equipped kitchen, barbeque area & swimming pool. 15 minutes drive from the Cape Town Int. airport and all the most popular beaches and mountain walks, City & V&A Waterfront. 5-10 Mins to University of Cape Town and schools.
neighborhood_overview:
Rondebosch is most centrally situated. Our accommodation is within easy access of the M5. The top Southern Suburb schools and hospitals are within a 5-15 minute drive, as is University of Cape Town. The Rondebosch, Mowbray and Royal Cape Golf Courses are within a 10 minute drive as is the Virgin Active Health Club. There are shopping malls and top restaurants in close proximity.
The Baxter Theatre 5 minutes away and the Artscape Theatre a 15-20 minute drive. Theatre on the Bay 20-30 minutes away.
The Atlantic and False Bay beaches are within a 15-20 minute drive. Easy access to bird watching and bird sanctuaries. Table mountain walks and the famous Kirstenbosch Botanical gardens. The Constantia wine route a mere 10 minute drive away.
The famous Stellenbosch Wine Route is 20-30 minutes. (A driver can be arranged at an extra fee).
The beautiful Franschhoek Valley and Hermanus (for Whale watching) are an easy absolutely picturous 75 minute drive.

host_about:
My husband and I thoroughly enjoy meeting people and hosting guests in Cape Town. We can offer advice on many alternative spots to visit and when the best time is to experience a Table Mountain or Cape Point visit. 

White Shark Diving trips can also be arranged on your behalf as well as guided hiking outings, at an extra cost.

description: Superb 2 bedroom penthouse, with balcony, in the heart of the city with free secured parking and unlimited fiber internet.  This apartment is the perfect base for both business and leisure visitors. Minimum 7 nights over Christmas and New Year.
host_about:

comments:
This is a fabulous apartment, lovely location and well kitted out. Very comfortable furniture, new sofa and chairs and the outside space has beautiful wooden chairs covered with padded, expeñsive looking cushions, we sat on the balcony most mornings with a coffee looking at the ships passing by and spotted 2 whales. Bed is huge and comfortable, lots of wardrobe space. Kitchen has everything you need, we were there 3 months and cooked regularly. Great coffee shop opposite, cafes, bars and restaurants closeby. The V&A is 20 min walk, Sea Point about 45 min walk, both accessible by the bus that stops outside or cheap Uber. Stellenbosch and Constantia are about 30 minutes away by Uber depending on traffic. Gordon or his local associates are on hand for any queries and respond quickly, although our queries were general as we didnt have any problems.
We loved every minute and have booked for 3 months next year.
name: Cape Town Seafront 1 Bedroom apartment
description: Popular cosy apartment on the seafront, lots of sunshine, sliding doors onto the balcony for an open outdoors feel, great views, quiet yet close to everything: safe for long walks along the seafront. Close to popular Waterfront with shops/restaurants
host_about:
I'm originally from Namibia, studied in South Africa and lived for a while in Cape Town before moving to London. I work as an Emergency Physician, mostly night shifts, sleeping by day, therefor often difficult to respond to booking requests and other questions immediately.

A magic place in a wonderful garden. Perfect for having a calm time. Scarborough is such a beautiful village and the beach and surroundings are perfect for nature lovers and any outdoor activities.
There is a good internet connection with speed in between 30 and 80 Mbps.
Thanks for having us!
name: Seaview cottage in Scarborough
description: A simple, stylish cottage with views of the Atlantic and National Park, and set in a fynbos garden. There is fibre optic WiFi, DSTV, a wind-sheltered braai area, a wooden deck and access to our solar-heated, salt water pool. The village is beautiful, quiet and friendly.
neighborhood_overview:
Scarborough boasts one of the most beautiful beaches on the whole Cape Peninsula, with a world-class surf break all year long, and graced by whales during the South African Spring.

There are endless walks in the hills behind us and along the coast, and a friendly community living here.

There's a shop selling organic produce in the village and two homely restaurants, and well-stocked supermarkets are not too far away.

Simon's Town and its penguins, and Kalk Bay and its fishing harbor - where you can buy fresh fish off the boats - are just over the hill, and we are en route to the famed Cape of Good Hope. Cape Town itself is a 40 minute drive away.
host_about: My wife and I have lived in Scarborough, near Cape Town, for 20 years. I am a journalist, and she is a therapist. We love good music, good company, good food, the many pleasures of Scarborough, and being within striking distance of Cape Town. Between us, we speak English, Zulu, Xhosa and French.

name: Cape Point Mountain Getaway - Cottage
description:
This is one of Cape Town's environmental and historic treasures. It is a candle-lit hideaway with stunning views of mountain and sea. The Cottage is completely off grid, with fresh water coming off the mountain and energy from the sun.

The cottage is built from local materials - stone walls, reed ceilings, blue gum supports. There are glass doors and windows throughout the cottage.

The cottage has a beautiful open-plan bedroom and bathroom. The bathroom includes a tub, a toilet and a basin.
neighborhood_overview:
Simonstown - 8km away
Cape Point Nature Reserve Gates - 4 km away
Restaurant - The Black Marlin - 2.3 km away
Swimming Beach at Castle Rock - 400 metres away
Sea - directly below you
You are located in the heart of Castle Rock Conservancy
Nearest neighbours - 250 metres away
host_about:
Conservationists & Green Warrior

We are passionate about the planet and how we impact it less. The house reflects this, being totally off the grid. We loved creating an oasis here to share with others - not only have we restored this locally famous home, we have removed all invasive plants so that the local flora & fauna thrives.

We love people, travel and books! People say I am larger than life ... I love life and embrace all it throws my way!

name: Big Bay Homestay La Paloma
description: Enjoy the beautiful Bloubergstrand ~ Cape Town, while staying in the tranquil setting of the La Paloma estate.  2 Bedrooms with separate entrance and sectioned off from our main home.  We look forward to welcome you to our home.
neighborhood_overview:
Big Bay Homestay is situated in the tranquil sought-after La Paloma Estate in Big Bay, Bloubergstrand. Walking distance to the beach with the stunning Table Mountain views. The Estate is also next to the Blaauwberg Nature Conservation area which adds to the natural beauty that one experience, staying on the West Coast.

This is a popular area amongst Wind and Kite Surfers, Stand up paddles, and traditional surfers. The long stretch of white sandy beach makes it ideal for relaxing beach walks. Smaller beaches in our area that can be found in the Blaauwberg Conservation Area between Big Bay and Melkbos, are Kreeftebaai and Haakgat.

Conveniently located less than 2 km away from Seaside Village and Eden on The Bay. Each center offers a variety of shops and restaurants. Surf Schools and Gear Rental shops can be found at Big Bay. If you are a keen golfer, make sure to visit Atlantic Beach Golf Estate situated only 5km from La Paloma, Big Bay.
host_about:
I love Cape Town and I am passionate to share my beautiful home and surroundings with visitors of Cape Town.  The most rewarding is when our guests leave with good memories and with a plan to return in the near future.

I manage our family guesthouse together with my dad and has been in the tourism industry since 1995.

I decided to expand my hospitality options, by now also offering a more private semi self-catering Homestay option at my home in La Paloma, Big Bay, Bloubergstrand. 

I do this with my husband Warren.  By staying with us we give guests the opportunity to experience the true lifestyle and culture of a South African family.  We love to meet people from all over the world and we love to share all our knowledge of our beautiful Cape Town and surrounds.  

Other interest:  
Art - I enjoy creating oil on canvas paintings.  Currently doing this as a hobby but would love to start selling my art work.
Muaythai - I am a retired fighter and still enjoy keeping fit by practicing fight orientated exercises.  I also enjoy teaching others how to Box. Guests can book a beach boxing session with Madeleine.
Food - Our family enjoys entertaining and cooking.

name: Rockwell 2bed, 2bath, Waterkant,  high floor&views
description:
Beautiful, Spacious and Secure, well decorated 2 bedroom, 2 bathroom, one en suite in the sought after Rockwell in de Waterkant. It is on the 5th floor with view over the foreshore, waterfront, the harbour and the ocean.

The Waterkant is one of the most sought after areas of Cape Town and it feels like a village within the big town with the CBD, Artscape, CTICC and Stadium accessible within minutes.
neighborhood_overview:
The best neighbourhood in town!

De Waterkant is a sub-district of the suburb of Green Point. An extremely central location tucked between Table Mountain, the bustling city centre and the prestigious V&A Waterfront; and neighboured by the historically coloured district of Bo-Kaap (in truth, De Waterkant is a modernised rendition of the Bo-Kaap).

The mixed Georgian, Cape Dutch and Asian colourful architecture, cobbled streets and historical standing is what makes De Waterkant so unique. The bright, 18th century cottages offer authentic ‘local’ living, nestling on the lower slopes of Signal Hill overlooking Table Bay.

De Waterkant is often described as being arty, charismatic and quaint and is a truly special place decorated with gorgeous al fresco restaurants, sidewalk cafés, boutique shops and bars; and is also home to the Cape Quarter shopping mall (all within walking distance).

It is often compared with Greenwich Village in New York.

comments:
Our stay with Karis and her family was delightful. It’s a small but very adequate space; we enjoyed lovely conversations with both Karis and Seb and their sons; and we found it a relaxing base for seeing much of Cape Town and environs. We loved getting acquainted at the local restaurant, Kushty’s, feeling we got not only delicious food and wonderful servers but a taste of the neighborhood too.. Walking around and through Green Point Park to the harbor and along the sea is another bonus. Listening to sea sounds including a morning fog horn and bird song was also lovely. Cape Town is unforgettable and our “nest” at Karis’s home was perfect for us!
Julie and Jim Aageson, Portland, Oregon

name: Market House, Flat 402.
description: It is a fourth floor flat within a building complex encompassing a bedroom, kitchen, toilet and shower. The kitchen is fully fitted which leads to the balcony that overlooks the sights and sounds of Greenmarket Square.
neighborhood_overview: Within the heart of the city, where we are situated, it has a vibrant atmosphere and it is filled with tourists, virtually, throughout the year. This is within five minutes walk of the apartment.

name: Llandudno Beachway Apartment, steps from the beach
description:
The apartment is located just steps from Llandudno beach and is attached to the main house with private off street parking and includes a separate entrance to private garden and pool courtyard.
Accommodation includes large living area and kitchenette, outdoor patio and pool for private use, a full shared family bathroom, and a guest loo.

neighborhood_overview: Our neighbourhood is unique, as everyone interacts in the street meeting and chatting.  We are a close community who look after each other. The flatlet has it's own private alarm......with beams, so in summer you can even sleep with the doors open, plus electric fencing.

name: Spacious studio with sunny deck in Tamboerskloof
description: THE FRANGIPANI is a colourful open-plan studio + kitchenette + en-suite bathroom + 9m² balcony with views towards Signal Hill. Sleeping 2 people comfortably, it's a quiet haven just minutes away from busy tourist attractions and fabulous restaurants. We have a solar system including panels and a solar geyser, as well as an internet backup battery that protects us from loadshedding (unless it's days and days of blackouts).
neighborhood_overview:
Tamboerskloof is a neighbourhood of Cape Town, South Africa. It lies on the slopes of Lion's Head and Signal Hill, adjacent to the neighbourhoods of Gardens and Bo-Kaap. Tamboerskloof is one neighbourhood within the bouquet of neighbourhoods referred to as the City Bowl.
We are very centrally situated being only 10 minutes from the Waterfront and the CBD but also 10 minutes drive from the beautiful beaches of Camps Bay and Clifton.
Many restaurants, shops, bars and clubs a few minutes away.
A choice of hiking trails are literally on your doorstep - the slopes of Signal Hill are just metres away at the opposite end of our street.
Gym, golf, tennis, bowls and water sports can be enjoyed nearby
Table Mountain and the Signal Hill scenic Drive are just five minutes drive away.
New soccer stadium, V&A Waterfront, Cape Town City Centre just 7-10 minutes by car.
Conference Centre 5 minute drive away.
Clifton and Camps Bay 10 minutes away.
host_about:
I live with Chris, my longterm boyfriend, in our little piece of jungle 5 minutes away from Cape Town's CBD, one of the world's most beautiful cities.  We try to tread lightly on this earth, and ride scooters, have solar water heating and a little natural swimming pond that I'm particularly proud of.
The slopes of Table Mountain start at the end of the road; this is where you'll often find us with our dogs, either walking, running, cycling, fetching wood or chatting with friendly neighbours.

host_about:

name: Abracadabra Loft
description:
Flanked by Cape Town's famous monumental landmarks, "Abracadabra" is a luxurious loft at the foot of Lion's Head, midway between the popular W&A Waterfront and the breathtaking Camps Bay Beach.
Elevated, quiet and largely wind-free, the spacious loft is just a few minutes' walk from the trendy Kloofstreet with all its vibrant bars, restaurants and cafés. The four-poster bed with mosquito net ensures an undisturbed, peaceful sleep.
neighborhood_overview: Tamboerskloof is one of the historic areas of Cape Town, with lots of small houses that are protected monuments. The neighborhood in this district is very active and everywhere people are "getting together for a chat." Due to the nearby German school, many German-speaking families live here; Tamboerskloof is also jokingly called "Sauerkraut Hills." Many of the hottest bars, cafes and restaurants in Cape Town are within walking distance, making Tamboerskloof extremely popular with the younger generation. But this neighborhood is also special because the strong winds in summer, "the Cape Doctor," largely spare it. All in all, a family-friendly neighborhood, quiet but still close to all attractions.
host_about:
Moin Moin,

eigentlich lebten wir (mein Mann, 3 Kinder und ich) glücklich und zufrieden im schönen Flensburg. Doch hat uns - mal wieder - das Reisefieber und die Abenteuerlust gepackt und somit sind wir nun auf ungewisse Zeit in Kapstadt/Südafrika sesshaft geworden !!!!!! (Wer rastet - rostet;-)).

comments:
Die Unterkunft von Karin ist außergewöhnlich! Die Wohnung ist hell, sehr ruhig, super ausgestattet und unglaublich geschmackvoll eingerichtet. Es gab wirklich nichts was wir vermisst haben.

Wir würden sehr gerne wiederkommen!

neighborhood_overview:
Tamboerskloof is a neighbourhood of Cape Town, South Africa. It lies on the slopes of Lion's Head and Signal Hill, adjacent to the neighbourhoods of Gardens and Bo-Kaap. Tamboerskloof is one neighbourhood within the bouquet of neighbourhoods referred to as the City Bowl.
We are very centrally situated being only 10 minutes from the Waterfront and the CBD but also 10 minutes drive from the beautiful beaches of Camps Bay and Clifton.
Many restaurants, shops, bars and clubs a few minutes away.
A choice of hiking trails are literally on your doorstep - the slopes of Signal Hill are just metres away at the opposite end of our street.
Gym, golf, tennis, bowls and water sports can be enjoyed nearby
Table Mountain and the Signal Hill scenic Drive are just five minutes drive away.
New soccer stadium, V&A Waterfront, Cape Town City Centre just 7-10 minutes by car.
Conference Centre 5 minute drive away.
Clifton and Camps Bay 10 minutes away.
host_about:
I live with Chris, my longterm boyfriend, in our little piece of jungle 5 minutes away from Cape Town's CBD, one of the world's most beautiful cities.  We try to tread lightly on this earth, and ride scooters, have solar water heating and a little natural swimming pond that I'm particularly proud of.
The slopes of Table Mountain start at the end of the road; this is where you'll often find us with our dogs, either walking, running, cycling, fetching wood or chatting with friendly neighbours.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

name: Sunny & spacious home in hip neighborhood
description: Bright and spacious Victorian cottage in lively Observatory, Cape Town, featuring tropical gardens and a large modern bathroom. Enjoy cosy fireplaces, a fully equipped kitchen, and secure off-street parking. Just a 10-minute drive to the City Centre, with easy access to public transport. Walk to popular shops, restaurants, and bars that make this suburb so special. Perfect for guests seeking charm, comfort, and convenience in a unique location.
neighborhood_overview: Observatory (Obs) is a bohemian suburb with a broad mix of students, foreigners, long-term residents and holiday makers.  It is one of the more colorful and vibey of Cape Town's suburbs and has an energy that you often won't find elsewhere.  The suburb is an "improvement district", that provides additional private security patrols and, for those who want it, escorts home from the main bars and restaurants at night.
host_about: Married with two adult children. Love the outdoors and a keen cyclist and runner. Classic Motorcar enthusiast and love Adventure Motorcycling (BMW GS). My favorite travel destination is Italy (Io amo l'Italia) and love Pasta's & Pizza. Cape town is probably the Jazz capital of Africa and needless to say my love for Jazz music is a natural persuasion. I'd like to believe that I have the gift of hospitality simply because people matter to me and it is very important that i can help them have the best experience of our country. My style of travelling is definitely flying but I have a dream to travel around the country in an RV (Recreational Vehicle) or Camper. My life motto is "Nothing is impossible"!

name: Sensational Views from Springbok Rd in Cape Town
description:
A one of a kind, sprawling Cape Town villa that offers jaw dropping views of the Green Point commons and the iconic Cape Town Stadium against a backdrop of the Atlantic Ocean and Robben Island.

Ideally located in Green Point, on the Atlantic Seaboard in one of CT's most exclusive & secure streets within minutes of the City Centre, all grocery stores, the V&A Waterfront, most tourist attractions & CT amenities, beaches & hiking paths.
neighborhood_overview:
The house is very conveniently located on the foot of Signal Hill in the Cape Town suburb of Green Point. Green Point is very conveniently located between the city center and the nearest point of the Atlantic Seaboard ocean. It is perhaps the most conveniently situated house for as house with such a great view.

name: Lido Beach Front Apartment with Sea Views
description: Lido is North facing - the best way to face in Cape Town. It is warm with sunlight during the cooler months and fresh with a sea breeze during Summer. The location on the Beach Front is situated in a bend enjoying a similar climate to Bantry Bay being wind protected. The trendy Mojo Market, Point Shopping Centre and many fantastic eateries and coffee shops are within 1 street from the apartment.
neighborhood_overview: Located on the Beach front promenade and opposite The Pavilion salt water swimming pools and many beaches. The Mojo food market, Point Shopping Centre and many new coffee shops and restaurants are located one street from the apartment. A 5 minute drive to Bantry Bay and Clifton. The “Hop On” Red Bus is located a few minutes away. Bicycle and Scooter hire is situated close by as well as 3 of the main supermarket chains.

description: An "African Chic" Studio with stunning ever-changing sea views and enjoying spectacular sea life and sunsets.  Modern interiors with luxury African furnishings.  Ideal location right on the Mouille Point "Platinum Mile" facing the Atlantic Ocean and Robben Island.
neighborhood_overview: Located in one of the most central and beautiful positions in Cape Town.  The building is located in front of the new Cape Town Stadium facing the Atlantic Ocean and Robben Island.  The Mouille Point promenade stretches along the Atlantic Seaboard and is unique consisting of a row of buildings and the beautiful Urban Park located directly behind.  The neighbourhood is safe and clean in walking distance to upmarket restaurants and coffee shops.  The Virgin Active Gym, the V & A Waterfront and the Cape Town Stadium is located on your doorstep.  The suburb is not overly developed and the freshness of the ocean is empowering.  A few minutes drive to the City Centre, Green Point, Table Mountain, Clifton, Camps Bay and all tourist attractions that Cape Town has to offer.

name: Magnificent Table Mountain views
description: Bedroom in beautiful apartment in the heart of Cape Town’s oldest character suburb Tamboerskloof, just below iconic Lions Head, stunning views of Table Mountain & the lively city. When staying in Cape Town it's all about LOCATION!!!!
host_about:
I am a great host, and will recommend places for you to visit, great local spots and events that most tourists don't get to know about in the guide books and will answer any questions you might have about the city and help you have as much fun as possible while visiting. 
I'm a social butterfly and can usually recommend something interesting to do while in Cape Town. 
I love my city and tend to squeeze the life out of it with mountain hikes, plenty of beach time, concerts in the park, dinning and exploring the local Art scene.
I am well traveled and have a passion learning from new cultures and can speak english, german, afrikaans and spanglish.

THE COTTAGE
The cottage has been recently renovated to a high standard to create a unique, light and modern open space with a blend of European and African influences. Photovoltaic system installed in 2023/ No power outage!

name: Stylish and bright Sea Point 1-Bed apartment
description: Perfect for a couple on holidays in Cape Town, and all set up for remote working, this light, stylish and super-comfortable one bedroom apartment is in fashionable Sea Point – a great and convenient location for visiting all the sights with easy access to the city centre, gorgeous beaches & trendy V&A Waterfront. A bus stop is right outside, while fabulous restaurants and all the shops you will need are just a short walk away.
neighborhood_overview:
Ocean-facing Sea Point is a lively, affluent suburb on the slopes of Signal Hill just outside the city centre between Mouille Point, Green Point and Bantry Bay. Just about everything you need is found in this part of the city and it hums with a cosmopolitan vibe.

It is divided by Main Road, with its many shops, supermarkets, coffee shops, sushi bars, fast-food eateries, pizzerias and fine restaurants. African handicrafts, craft beer and live music are among the draws at the colourful Mojo Market.

The promenade runs the length of Sea Point’s beachfront and is a hub of activity, particularly in the late afternoons to watch the sunset over the Atlantic. Reaching the trendy and there-to-be-seen beaches of Clifton and Camps Bay is an easy walk or drive, and the city centre and the V&A Waterfront are minutes away.

name: Cosy safe Panorama, solar, with 233Mbps Wi-Fi
description:
This centrally located, fully equipped self-catering suite has easy access to the City centre, 3 beach fronts, shopping malls, Grand West Casino, Canal Walk, Tygervalley Centre , restaurants and wine farms. The Panorama Medi Clinic is 1km away.
Suitable for digital nomads, Portuguese speaking, business travellers and families, 2 bedrooms and 2 en suite bathrooms and a private outdoor area, ideally for breakfast.
neighborhood_overview:
The neighbourhood is quiet, safe and friendly.
We have very well-known restaurants close to us that serve seafood, steaks, pasta and sushi.
For shopping, Canal Walk at Century City with a lot of top shoe and fashion boutiques also at the V & A Waterfront are 15 minutes drive away with top class restaurants. Tyger Valley Shopping Mall is close by.
De Grendal, Meerendal, Diemersdal and Durbanville Hills is a short drive away as well as the Stellenbosch wine route.
We are also in the middle of Big Bay, Table View and Blaauwberg Strand for surfing and kite surfing as well as Clifton, Camps Bay, Strand and Gordons Bay.
host_about: I am a South African lady married to a Portuguese gentleman.  We like to meet and travel and meet people from different countries. It is important for us to make sure our guests feel at home.

name: A Loft Suspended Between Mountain and Sea
description:
A unique property with the best views on the coast - the sea on one side and the mountain on the other. A spacious loft under the rafters of a solid and charming Edwardian bungalow. Sunlit, serene, spacious, stylish and comfortable. Great beds, 100% cotton bedding, luxury bathroom, kitted out kitchen. 5 min walk from the village.

WE WELCOME DIGITAL NOMADS!
- Excellent, stable wifi
- Dedicated work table
- Always electricity & wifi, even during load-shedding (inverter)
neighborhood_overview: You will fall in love with Kalk Bay, if you haven't already - an atmospheric village only 30 minutes drive from central Cape Town, of artists and writers and fishing families; of restaurants, bakeries, craft shops, and the glorious fishing harbour; of history and charm. We buy our fresh fish  from the harbour, and our daily bread from the world-famous Olympia bakery, which also serves great coffee and has one of the Cape's best restaurants.  There are at least a dozen restaurants, bars and cafes within easy walking distance, and two  extraordinary independent bookshops in the village, Kalk Bay Books and Kwagga Books, owned by our neighbours, Audrey and George. There are two magnificent tidal pools to swim in - Wooley's Pool just below the house, and Dalebrook in the village - and Fish Hoek Beach is only 20 min walk away. comments: We had a wonderful stay and Mark was a very friendly, knowledgeable and helpful host with lots of great local insight. The flat is very private, well equipped, light and airy, even on the hotter days. The views are beautiful and there are lots of cafés, restaurants and tidal pools just a short walk away. We would definitely return and highly recommend!

name: Cape Point Mountain Getaway - Villa
description: A magnificent historical home surrounded by fynbos, overlooking False Bay with stunning sea views and an imposing mountain backdrop. The villa is located in a nature conservancy. It is completely off the grid: solar energy, water from a mountain stream. This place is for people who want beauty and tranquillity and  a getaway experience in a 100% environmentally friendly place right on the urban edge - 8kms from Simonstown.  Fully equipped open plan kitchen, exquisite bedrooms and awesome decks.
neighborhood_overview: The villa is in a nature conservancy. The nearest urban area, Simon's Town, is 8 kilometres away. The nearest houses are 400 metres down the road. The sea is directly below the house but the nearest beach with easy access is less than a kilometre away. The gates to Cape Point Nature Reserve are 4 kilometres to the South. You share the Getaway with one other dwelling, a small cottage that is completely separate and tucked into the trees and the mountainside
host_about:
Conservationists & Green Warrior

We are passionate about the planet and how we impact it less. The house reflects this, being totally off the grid. We loved creating an oasis here to share with others - not only have we restored this locally famous home, we have removed all invasive plants so that the local flora & fauna thrives.

We love people, travel and books! People say I am larger than life ... I love life and embrace all it throws my way!

name: Bo Kaap Garden Apartment 3
description:
Gorgeous apartment set in a magical secret garden on Chiappini street in Bo Kaap, in Cape Town's city centre. Enjoy peace and tranquility, rich cultural heritage and walking distance to all the City Centre has to offer. We offer discounts for longer stays during winter months, and for very long stays over the summer; please send through a request if you'd like a lengthy stay.

Also, good news! We don't get load shed :)
neighborhood_overview:
Bo Kaap is the best place to stay in Cape Town. Wander the cobbled streets, chat with friendly neighbours, and enjoy the bests of both worlds -- quiet, community feel yet just a five minute walk into the city centre.

The property is situated on the famous Chiappini street, with its cobbled roads and brightly coloured houses. The street has a vibrant vibe and a very diverse mix of people. The house is very secure and quiet, aside from the call to prayer from nearby mosques. The property is situated just between the Longmarket Street mosque, and the old quarry which borders an informal settlement. Cars are parked in the street and it's best not to leave anything in the car, as left items in cars has led to break-ins in the past.

There are a lot of great restaurants and bars within walking distance; we recommend:
Bocca - cnr Wale & Bree - delicious food & cocktails
Clarkes - Bree St - diner that does great breakfasts and an awesome clam chowder)
Char

name: Bo Kaap Garden Apartment 3
description:
Gorgeous apartment set in a magical secret garden on Chiappini street in Bo Kaap, in Cape Town's city centre. Enjoy peace and tranquility, rich cultural heritage and walking distance to all the City Centre has to offer. We offer discounts for longer stays during winter months, and for very long stays over the summer; please send through a request if you'd like a lengthy stay.

Also, good news! We don't get load shed :)
neighborhood_overview:
Bo Kaap is the best place to stay in Cape Town. Wander the cobbled streets, chat with friendly neighbours, and enjoy the bests of both worlds -- quiet, community feel yet just a five minute walk into the city centre.

The property is situated on the famous Chiappini street, with its cobbled roads and brightly coloured houses. The street has a vibrant vibe and a very diverse mix of people. The house is very secure and quiet, aside from the call to prayer from nearby mosques. The property is situated just between the Longmarket Street mosque, and the old quarry which borders an informal settlement. Cars are parked in the street and it's best not to leave anything in the car, as left items in cars has led to break-ins in the past.

There are a lot of great restaurants and bars within walking distance; we recommend:
Bocca - cnr Wale & Bree - delicious food & cocktails
Clarkes - Bree St - diner that does great breakfasts and an awesome clam chowder)
Char

name: PingusHouse in Simons Town, Private Pool & Garden
description:
Dine on the terrace overlooking trees to the ocean. Floor-to-ceiling windows bring the outside in at this tranquil, open-plan home with sleek interiors. Unwind outside in the private garden and solar-heated pool, or wander down to the nearby beach.
The house has 4 bedrooms, 3 in the main house and one outside the main building, within the property.
neighborhood_overview: The home is located in Simon’s Town near Boulders Beach in a quiet, safe area that's just metres from secluded white sands. Watch the Boulders National Park penguins on the rocks. Grocery shops, restaurants, and coffee shops are all nearby.
host_about:
Thierry & Mary Banchet

We are a French-Spanish family that moved from Europe to South Africa 12 years ago. We now live near Mossel Bay in a Game Reserve. Besides from wildlife we also love diving so we were very happy when we discovered this wonderful place in Simon's Town with incredible experiences for diving with sharks, seals, near to the penguins and whales! And it is a lovely and quiet town. PingusHouse is now our holiday/diving house, and our daughter loves to go to the little beaches near the house and also swim with the penguins at Boulders beach, when we are able to be there. From time to time we also enjoy some city life, so the house is not so far from cape town city center.

name: Hout Bay Child-Friendly Beach Cottage
description:
Let the children run free in the fully enclosed garden, with its tropical plants and mountain views. Return by the French doors to discover that this family-friendly home has much else to offer, including an indoor fireplace and beach-style decor.

The house is North-West facing making it sunny and light. The Cape Summer wind comes to the rear of the cottage, which means the front is nicely sheltered from this famous South Easter wind, this is a big plus if you stay in Cape Town!

Hout Bay Beach Cottage has a fully enclosed private garden
offering a safe environment for children and an oasis for adults,
close to everything yet just far enough from the main road to
ensure guests can enjoy the tranquillity of the garden and the
beautiful mountain views.

The garden offers various seating options for relaxation;
A hammock, 4 sunloungers with pillows and a large umbrella, outdoor dining area with weber braai and a modern outdoor pa
neighborhood_overview:
Stroll just 100 meters from the door to reach Hout Bay beach. It's also within walking distance of the harbour, not to mention restaurants and shops. The harbour offers daily boat trips to seal island, shops and restaurants. There are many activities in Hout Bay, such as surfing, kayaking, peddle boarding, snorkeling with seals, beautiful hikes, electric bike and motorbike rentals, visit to the World of Birds (largest bird park in Africa) and more.
To explore further afield, simply catch the famous red 'hop on and off' bus around the corner. This citysightseeing bus has various routes on offer and stops at all major tourist locations in Cape Town, including a Cape Point line which stops at the penguins in Simon's Town, Boulders beach as well.

name: Cosy Kloofstreet 1BD with Balcony and City Views
description: Our Sunny, cozy, Romantic little 1bedroom apartment is located on the foot of Table Mountain and is situated right at the top of one of Cape Town City's most famous and beautiful streets, Kloofstreet. Ideal for couples and remote workers who want to explore the city!
neighborhood_overview:
Kloof street, Gardens is probably one of the most popular areas in Cape Town city bowl.Take a walk down this lovely street and you'll quickly realise why.

If dining out is your thing, you're in the right place. Kloof street is densely populated with some of the best restaurants Cape Town has to offer.
For nature lovers, Table mountain National park is a 5min taxi or bus ride up the hill. Take the Cable car up to Table Mountain's peak or go for a sunset hike up Lion's head.
The city centre is a 1km walk down Kloof street. Alternatively you can make use of Cape Town's brand new public transport system to explore the city and surrounding areas.
host_about:
My name is Stefan and I'm a full-time touring musician, My Girlfriend Anke is from Belgium and we’d love to host you at our beautiful apartment in Cape Town.

We love our little apartment in Cape Town. We love it so much that we hate to leave it but as a touring musician, I need to travel all the time. Anke usually joins me on tour during school holidays and we feel that it's such a shame to leave such a perfect spot in this beautiful city abandoned. So we have decided to share our little piece of Joy with the world.


Please don't hesitate to contact us if you'd like to find out about availability of our apartment. :-)

name: Inn Africa Beachfront Self Catering
description:
Inn Africa self catering is located 1 road back from beautiful un-spoilt beaches, with stunning 180' views of Table Mountain, Robben Island and the beautiful Atlantic Ocean (most photographed views in South Africa) the unit is 200m walk from the famous Blouberg kitesurfing beach & tidal pools It is situated between Bloubergstrand and Big Bay, and 2km from supermarkets, restaurants, pubs and cafe's.
An ideal place for holiday-makers and business travellers alike.
No pets allowed.
neighborhood_overview:
We are situated in a lovely upmarket area in the heart of Bloubergstrand. Blouberg is famous for it's kite-surfing beach & tidal pools, and the view of Table Mountain across the bay. It is situated between Bloubergstrand and Big Bay, and 2 km from supermarkets, restaurants, pubs and cafe's. Cape Town
Central is 20km away and takes approximately 20-25 mins during off peak traffic time.

name: Braemar Villa
description: Old style family home in the funky fishing village of Kalk Bay. The house has 4 bedrooms and sleeps up to 8 people. It is located above the harbour with stunning views and is walking distance from restaurants , coffee shops, the beaches and pools.
neighborhood_overview:
Stay in the best village anywhere with beautiful tidal pools and beaches, excellent coffee and fine dining as well as interesting shopping all accessible on foot from Braemar Villa. Lever street park is great for dogs, kids and anyone who enjoys a good coffee! The active fishing harbour is a delight and fresh fish can be purchased directly from the local fisherman. Alternatively explore an array of culinary experiences from fine dining to fish and chips on the harbour wall
The neighborhood is unique in South Africa as one of the few places where apartheid policies never relocated " people of color " leaving the local fisherman ,who are of Cape Malay descent, in Kalk Bay. They first set up the fishing community here in the mid 1800's.
host_about: We love living in the south of Cape Town and enjoying all it has to offer especially swimming in the ocean and mountain walks.

name: Cute cottage in quiet cul-de-sac
description: Cottage is located in the quirky "village" of Hout Bay - surrounded by magnificent mountains. HB offers an eclectic range of restaurants and bars.  The Sentinel Mountain provides an iconic backdrop to the HB Harbour.  Llandudno beach 10 mins away
neighborhood_overview: Hout Bay is a"quirky" suburb of Cape Town's Atlantic Seaboard - it is not definitely not "suburbia"!  Hout Bay boasts interesting art galleries, fabulous restaurants and bars, boat trips to Seal Island, wonderful hikes in the surrounding mountains and forest walks.
host_about: Eugene, my husband and I are born and bred Capetonians and have been hosting guests in our cottage for over twenty years.  We love meeting people and most of our guests become friends who often return!  I have been retired for a few years and loving it!  I had worked in the hospitality industry (at the Ambassador and V&A Hotel, Waterfront, Cape Town) for almost 40 years.  We host pub/restaurant quizzes, as well as corporate events.  We are passionate about quizzing!  See photo taken when we were on the TV game show "Who Wants to be a Millionaire" a few years ago.  We had fun and did quite well - our 15-minutes of fame!  We love entertaining - especially outdoors.   And we are animal lovers - all doggie people are most welcome ... We have a beautiful old rescue dog (Bella) as well as a mini schnauzer (Nezzi)!

comments: Fabulous and comfortable.
name: Uber Luxe Apartment near Saunders Rock Bantry Bay
description:
Escape to this luxurious apartment just a minute away from two conveniently situated (2minutes away) beaches and gorgeous new Bantry Bay restaurant & deli (Stepbros)

We’ll “have you at hello” from the glamorous chandelier foyer through to the nautical-themed master bedroom and elegant lounges. Each room is meticulously styled to combine flair with function - making for a really fun stay. The objective is to surprise & delight Guests to enjoy a unique experience away from home..

Each section of the apartment is themed and styled for function and aesthetics albeit with a slightly playful nature... Downstairs bedroom is Tom Ford-inspired (just in case he would need a place to stay when visiting Cape Town - I remain eternally hopeful...)

Kitchen is remnant of Belgium's signature Black & White Contemporary Shaker Style, whilst the open-plan lounge is clearly an ode to Ralph Lauren. Upstairs is Hamptonesque / Cape Cod-ish. Navy & White delight. The Space i
neighborhood_overview: The apartment is located in the exclusive wind-free suburb of Bantry Bay. The President Hotel is right next door & it is less than a minute’s walk to Saunders Beach, the Promenade, MyCiti & fabulous new restaurant & deli (Stepbrothers) at AURUM LUXE
host_about:
In a nutshell: I'm a Jhb-Girl, now living in Cape Town. I love beautiful things and believe that true comfort is the ultimate in luxury. 

I'm a Style Director and Designer and have had loads of fun creating these two "International Lifestyle Apartments". Every item of soft furnishing & all style items have been thought through, tried & tested for guest comfort & fun. I am unashamedly inspired by the work of Tom Ford & Ralph Lauren. They understand that form & function work hand-in-hand and result in memorable stayovers.  

Both my apartments contain elements of classic style, uber-comfort & a hint of modernity.  They are filled with plenty of reading matters and entertainment essentials. I'm a believer in old-fashioned values amidst our modern society. When traveling, one's accommodation plays a huge part of the overall trip experience. Add fantastic dining experiences & excursions from the in-house guidebook and you're all set for a fail-safe holiday! Who doesn’t enjoy "next level"  everything...

I love being part of the Airbnb Society and realise how precious & limited a well-deserved holiday or business trip break is to each individual. It takes so little to add a whole lot more to someone's experience. 

When good Hosts and Lovely Guests unite, we create magic together. Our memories define us... LA x

name: 1920s Cape Town beach cottage, Clovelly/Kalk Bay
description: Simple, comfortable, family-friendly, 1920s beach cottage in Clovelly, 100m from Fish Hoek beach with life saver, shark nets and cafe, 10 mins walk from cool Kalk Bay with harbour, great restaurants and shops.  Situated on edge of nature reserve, surfing and golf within 2 miles, CT city 20 minutes drive. Wraparound verandah, large beautiful garden. Secure, accessible (road and rail), and well set up for loadshedding (battery backup for wifi, outside gas cooking station, recharging light bulbs).
neighborhood_overview:
This eastern side of Cape Town has warm seas from the Indian ocean - unlike the freezing Atlantic on the west of Cape Town which is too cold for swimming. The house is in Clovelly, and on Clovelly Road, and there is a lovely golf course just a mile inland.
The cottage faces a wetland nature reserve with great birdlife, and which is also home to the endangered Western Leopard Toad. The reserve has pathways which are perfect for jogging or walking. There are many local runs on our local beach and coastal roads, which can be started from the front gate.
The beach is a brilliant, safe, family beach, with a shark net, and life savers. It is about 1km of excellent sandy beach with a cafe for coffee and meals at the end, a sailing club, and local fishermen bringing in the fishing boats daily. Round the corner is trendy Kalk Bay harbour with very good restaurants, pubs, antique and book shops, boutiques and so on. A couple of miles (and a few railway stations) further up the same
host_about: I am a university professor living in Norfolk in the UK. I come from South Africa and have family there, as well as my beach cottage in Cape Town, called Skemering after my grandparents beach house. I spend a couple of months in Cape Town every year, gardening, enjoying the beach and writing.

name: Octopus Garden Murdock Valley Simonstown. Capetown
description:
3 bedroom Simonstown holiday villa with incredible sea views and close to amenities fisherman's beache offers a tranquil and perfect holiday setting.
Great for a family or 2 - 3 couples wanting to get away for a summer or winter vacation in a lovely venue. Simonstown offers so much from beautiful beaches fishing spots hikes mountain bike rides. Kalkbay and Muizenberg minutes away. Some fabulous quaint village places surrounded by picturesque nature. Restful tranquility. A soulfood place.
neighborhood_overview: Simon's Town is a tranquil, beautiful and historic village set on the Southern Most tip of the Cape Peninsula. You wake up in the morning and look across the beautiful bay just as the sun peeks over the mountains. Walking down the road to the coast you encounter penguins waddling towards the clear, cool sea. Out of the corner of your eye you see three otters running along the beach and launching into the waves. Raising your eyes as you walk along, you see a Southern Right whale breeching for the pure joy of it. You must be in Simon’s Town. Just a short walk up the road and you are in the Table Mountain National Park. The Old Mule Track zig zags up through the pristine fynbos. Simon’s Town is primarily a Naval base and many a day one can watch frigates and submarines come and go.

name: 1 Bedroom Apartment in Secure complex
description: A fully furnished appartment in Boundry Road, Green point with 24hr security, close to V&A waterfront and within walking distance to many restaurants, bars and shops. It's 4 minutes drive to the city centre and 10 minutes to the beach front.
neighborhood_overview:
This is a small self-catering apartment located in a security complex, in the beautiful and eclectic suburb of Green Point. Situated in a very safe and central area, it is a great place to explore the city from.

The apartment consists of a bedroom with spacious built-in-cupboards, a modern en-suite bathroom, a fully equipped kitchen and an open-plan living area. It is equipped with laundry facilities, a dishwasher, a TV with DStv, under-floor heating and Wi-Fi access. Secure parking is available on the premises as well as a designated parking area for visitors.

There are bus stops near the property for cost-saving transport, however, the host can arrange transport in case of emergencies, and is always available to guests to provide tours or advice.

Hi I’m Kyle and I have comfortable little Greenpoint apartment in a security complex.
 I’m able to share what I’ve  learned over the many years living in this city like which areas are good for what and am happy to help where ever I can when hosting a guest.

name: Distinctive, Modern Cottage with Terrace Pool
description:
The Living areas are open plan with lots of natural light spilling in from large fold out doors. There is a feeling of an indoor-outdoor experience with the rooms flowing onto a deck in the front and a Tuscan courtyard at the back of the house. The courtyard is surrounded by greenery and the sound of a trickling water feature. The front of the house is decked with a sun terrace, pool and a verandah with beautiful views of Table Mountain.

The cottage was one of the original Cape Cottages in the area and recently renovated and designed by an Architect and Interior Designer who worked closely together to achieve all the comforts of modern living and while maintaining the warmth and charm of this characterful Cape Cottage.

All three bedrooms have beautifully modern ensuites, large windows and privacy from the warm inviting living space and kitchen.

We will meet and greet you personally and offer advice and guidance to make the most of your stay in the bea
neighborhood_overview: The cottage is walking distance to the vibrant restaurants of Kloof Street. For beachgoers, Camps Bay with white sand beaches is within reach—or just lie by the tranquil pool at home and enjoy the mountain views and the sounds of the birds.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

comments: The apartment is in a great location for reaching all the main attractions via car, and the sunset views from the terrace are incredible. The apartment has all the amenities you need, making you feel very at home, very quickly. Vicky was also very quick to respond to questions which was really appreciated. There was unfortunately building works happening at the building next door which were loud when sitting outside. Also, note that there is only air conditioning in the living room.
