name: Beautiful Victorian house with pool
description: Victorian house with pool close to beach promenade in Sea Point. The perfect location to enjoy and explore Cape Town and the Cape Peninsula.
neighborhood_overview:
Almost everything in town is less than 10 minutes away by car. However, Sea Point is probably the only area in Cape Town, where a car is not even necessary. We would, however, recommend renting one for those long road trips to the wine estates and other areas outside the city center.

A great variety of coffee shops, great restaurants, bars, shops, beaches, jogging tracks, hiking trails, parks, public swimming pools are all within easy walking distance. Beautiful Sea Point promenade is a 3 minutes walk away. For the adventure seekers, sea kayaking and paragliding can all be found in the neighbourhood. The Lions Head hiking path starts a short walk away and is a must, with stunning panoramic views over the city bowl and Camps Bay. Green Point with its great park, golf course and World Cup stadium, the lively colourful Waterkant and Bokaap area, vibey Long Street with its restaurants and buzzing nightlife and the famous V&A Waterfront are all close by and can be easily reached
host_about:
We are a South African/German family. 

We love traveling and have lived in many great cities, including Cape Town, London, Amsterdam, Singapore and are currently living in Berlin. We both work in the Design & Advertising industry, adore sailing and the oceans, enjoy good food and wine and try to spend as much time as possible in the Mother City.

neighborhood_overview:

Local life is casual and in the evening residents and holiday makers can be found enjoying sundowners at the many chic sidewalk cafés on the beachfront.

neighborhood_overview:
Vibrant Camps Bay is the most popular suburb of Cape Town and sun-worshipers, beach-goers and surfers alike flock to its sun-kissed sandy white beach to show off their skills or laze in the sun. Connoisseurs of fine food and drink can indulge their senses in the world-class restaurants, funky bistros and cocktail lounges lining Camps Bay Promenade. Choose from a wide range of seafood, sushi, local cuisine and excellent South African wines. If a dose of culture is what you are looking for, check out the program at the Theatre on the Bay.
It is only a few minutes‘ drive to the Table Mountain Cable Car Station or up to Signal Hill – Cape Town’s no. 1 favourite spot to sip sundowners and watch the magnificent sunsets on the Atlantic Ocean. It is only 10 minutes by car to Cape Town city centre, the V&A Waterfront, the Two Ocean’s Aquarium, the antiques dealers, bookshops, cafes and pawnshops on Long Street.
Camps Bay is the ideal starting point for excursions by car or bike to

name: Spacious light, single (Red Room) with sunny patio
description: A piece of home-from-home in a shared user-friendly apartment, just a hop skip and a jump away from your early morning swim, surf or evening beach walk in super friendly, relaxed home. Walk to the beach, surf break, mountain, Kalk Bay harbour, vlei, shops or train stations to town. Please read our house rules very carefully before making a booking.
neighborhood_overview:
Welcome to Muizenberg! Muizenberg is a seaside suburb in Cape Town. Here the beach is long and white, the waves are kind and sea is warmer and so is our hospitality! A shopping centre, pharmacy, library, surf shops, restaurants, cafes and coffee shops are all within walking distance from the house, as are the train stations on the line into the city centre and this allows you to be independent. You can shop for provisions at the local shopping centre, find out about surf/kite lessons at the many surf shops, buy arts and crafts in historical Palmer Road, eat the freshest fish and chips at the sea, enjoy slow breakfasts at one of the many trendy restaurants, cafes and coffee shops, experience the local vibe at our Thursday and Friday night Bluebird Market, limber up at a yoga studio and so much more - and all this within walking distance of the house!
This environment is just bursting with possibilities. We are outdoors people and really enjoy what this area has to offer us and
host_about:
I am in my 60s, happy, busy and free-spirited. I deeply love and am proud of my two strong, beautiful daughters both in their mid-30s. I love mountaineering, hiking, surfing and pottering around at home. I have a passion for Rock Art and have spent years exploring the shelters in the Western Cape, finding paintings and artefacts. I am a keen botaniser and know and love the "fynbos" biome around me. I’m happiest in the mountains, hiking, climbing or camping.

B-i-i-i-ig things in my life have been the birth of my daughters, climbing a 6000m peak in Peru, crossing the Atlantic in a yacht and being temporarily struck down by a neural disease. However, I constantly find wonder and amazement in the small things in life  - watching my plants grow, riding a glistening early morning wave, coming across an interesting shaped rock on my walks, listening to great young jazz musicians and their South African flavoured compositions...

I listen to a lot of music, especially jazz and have evolving current favourites. I am prepared to listen to any music if it pushes some boundaries or is daring or is beautiful and gives me gooseflesh.

When I read, I like non-fiction. "A Short History of Nearly Everything" reminds me that I am made of stardust and that I come from a long line of surviving ancestors, so I'd better be thankful for being given life as a human! But "Life of Pi" taught me that life is what you make of it, especially when it comes to survival. "The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time" taught me that there are many different ways to view and experience life. 

My philosophy in life is to care for the earth and be aware of my footprint.  I have grown an indigenous garden from nothing, planted many trees and grown some of my own veggies. 

My other special gift is what nature provides five minutes from my house - a lovely beach and surf break - and I can introduce someone to surfing! 

I'm happy to host just about anyone but particularly like people who are enthusiastic and thirsty for experience. I realise that at times that my home needs to be a quiet place for someone to recharge their batteries, so lying in the sun in a hammock or snuggling up in a cosy bed for days is also cool with me.

B-i-i-i-ig things in my life have been the birth of my daughters, climbing a 6000m peak in Peru, crossing the Atlantic in a yacht and being temporarily struck down by a neural disease. However, I constantly find wonder and amazement in the small things in life  - watching my plants grow, riding a glistening early morning wave, coming across an interesting shaped rock on my walks, listening to great young jazz musicians and their South African flavoured compositions...

I listen to a lot of music, especially jazz and have evolving current favourites. I am prepared to listen to any music if it pushes some boundaries or is daring or is beautiful and gives me gooseflesh.

When I read, I like non-fiction. "A Short History of Nearly Everything" reminds me that I am made of stardust and that I come from a long line of surviving ancestors, so I'd better be thankful for being given life as a human! But "Life of Pi" taught me that life is what you make of it, especially when it comes to survival. "The Curious Incident of the Dog in the Night-Time" taught me that there are many different ways to view and experience life. 

My philosophy in life is to care for the earth and be aware of my footprint.  I have grown an indigenous garden from nothing, planted many trees and grown some of my own veggies. 

My other special gift is what nature provides five minutes from my house - a lovely beach and surf break - and I can introduce someone to surfing! 

I'm happy to host just about anyone but particularly like people who are enthusiastic and thirsty for experience. I realise that at times that my home needs to be a quiet place for someone to recharge their batteries, so lying in the sun in a hammock or snuggling up in a cosy bed for days is also cool with me.

name: (3) Beachfront views of Table Mountain
description: Two bedrooms, one ensuite opening onto a large stack back door enclosed veranda, and a stones throw to the beach. Uncapped WiFi. WiFi in the apartment stays on during loadshedding. Including the Hotel's TV package: SuperSport 1; 2; 3 & 4 for world wide Sports coverage,  3 x Mnet Movie Channels, Sky News, E-TV, and SABC 1; 2 & 3. Please note that the TV signal/reception in this building isn’t good at all.
neighborhood_overview: Just inland from Milnerton through to Tableview is a protected wetland area and within this zone is a splendid 18 hole golf course, readily available to guests, and rather inexpensive too. The bird-life in this area is truly wonderful; flamingos, pelicans, gannets, kingfishers, egrets, herons and more! The city has recently laid out a wonderful bike road all the way along the coast from the city through to Table View and beyond; for cyclists, joggers or bird-lovers it is a rare treat. There is a decent surf break just a hundred meters along from the apartment and it is a good spot for learners. Board rentals and training are easily arranged. From Milnerton beach along to Table View is one of the world's most popular stretches of kite surfing surf and just watching the kites speed about or wondering at the cloud table cloth as it falls over the edge of Table Mountain is an unforgettable experience. 5km away is Canal Walk, the largest shopping mall in Africa and the Ratanga Junction Them
host_about: My business partner, Henry, and I,  host this and a few other Airbnbs here in Cape Town. We are available 24/7 online to help out with planning your stay and to problem solve while you are here in the Cape. We are both lifelong Capetonians and know the Cape intimately and will recommend only what we think is excellent and of good value. Whatever you want to accomplish while you are here, we will point you in the right direction. You will be in safe hands.

comments: Our two night stay at Rose Cottage was a real treat. Linda is a proud Superhost, and for good reason. The Rose Cottage is set in your own private tranquil garden. Birdsong is your morning wake up call.  You can take your steaming cup of coffee/tea to the patio and watch them scratch around the leafy garden beds looking for their breakfast while contemplating where your next adventure will be. The furnishings suit the cottage down to a tee and beds are comfortable and cozy. Loved the personal touch of a vase of fresh mini roses on the coffee table for us to enjoy.  Definitely a place to return to should we need to be in Cape Town again.
name: Rose Cottage, Constantia
description: Situated in the wine region of Constantia, our tastefully appointed and comfortable accommodation is surrounded by lush gardens with swimming pool and close to all that Cape Town has to offer. We are less than 20 minutes from the city and beaches.
host_about: Born in Cape Town, UCT graduate. Have two married sons, two grandsons and two granddaughters all living in the UK. I enjoy French and Italian conversation; gardening (propagate plants for sale); playing bridge; reading; cooking (have a Cordon Bleu certificate); travelling to see our family and visiting other new places. I love having guests to stay and try to make them feel very welcome. My life motto: live well, laugh often. Carpe Diem.

name: Central Private Secure with pool, garden, parking
description: Our studio flat is at the foot of Table Mountain, on public transport routes, 4km from the City Centre. The open plan configuration has a queen bed (182cm*152cm), a well-equipped kitchen, private garden with barbecue, private entrance, secure off-street parking, Wi-Fi, Netflix, and alarm. Guests may share our solar-heated swimming pool.
neighborhood_overview: Our neighbourhood of Highlands Estate, Vredehoek has the immediate advantage of being close to Table Mountain. A short 50m walk will lead you into the foothills of the mountain from where you can mountain bike or hike, including right to the top of Table Mountain. I am a regular hiker and am always happy to have guests accompany me on my walks in the mountain. comments: Talitha's studio is fantastic: extremely clean, bright, with a lovely balcony and a very comfortable bed. The kitchen is fully equipped with everything needed for cooking. The immediate area is quiet, the sea is very close, and great Sea Point restaurants and bars are within walking distance. name: Excellent Location, Ideal Apartment in Cape Town
description: Wonderful stylish studio apartment in the most popular & trendy,  Atlantic Seaboard. The most central location in Cape Town.  A Light & bright interior space opening out to a  large Sunny balcony, a place to sit in the sun & enjoy the views. A fully equipped,  kitchen, bathroom with shower.  Enjoy the super comfy Luxury king-size bed, unlimited wifi, select DStv /cable and one parking bay (none-secured)  Within easy walking distance to all amenities, the beach, mountains, a popular restaurants.
neighborhood_overview: This trendy neighborhood is one of Cape Town most sought after locations for Cape Towns local elite as well as visitors in the know.  It is super close to everything, from trendy restaurants, coffee shops and bars, to beaches and mountian trails.  A five minute walk  to the most beautiful promenades along the ocean where one can walk, run, or cycle for miles. Each morning and evening it is a delight of fit, healthy bodies running, walking and exercising along the strip. This same promenade, known as the Sea Point Promenade, leads to the WaterFront, a glorious combination of world class shopping and incredible restaurants, with ocean and mountian views. We are also within walking distance to the famous Soccer Stadium surrounded by beautiful parks, for your indulgence. We are on the bus route, and easy driving distance to Cape Town glorious beaches, sunset strip of Camps Bay, the city and Table Mountain. Three Anchor Bay is a trendy residential area, and an ideal place to spend a holiday
host_about: I have lived in Cape Town for a number of years and  adore it! The magical outdoors with its vast choice of beaches, mountians and consistent beauty is the heart and soul of Cape Town. A mix of cultures makes this rather unique, somewhat like the buzz of the city on the foothills of Table Mountian. One can be in the mountains and two minutes later sipping coffee at one of many trendy coffee shops. Added to the love of Cape Town, I love the mix of Greyton, one of the Western Cape's most popular towns. Nestled in the most beautiful valley, surrounded by mountains, a must for any local or visitor to pop into. After living abroad for years I came home to the most beautiful country in the world.

name: Your Tranquil and Airy Home beneath Lions Head
description:
The cozy, light-flooded and high-quality furnished 2.5 room apartment with its own green garden in the heart of the popular neighbourhood Tamboerskloof leaves nothing to be desired.

Quietly located with a view of the green Signal Hill and yet close to the trendy Kloof Street with the best restaurants, cafes and shops.

A lockable small covered parking space is available (if not reserved). The car port can fit cars up to a length of 430cm.
neighborhood_overview: The apartment is situated at the base of Signal Hill, sheltered on a quaint street and protected from the South Easter wind. There's access to nature, while also close to the vibrant city center of Cape Town. An effortless trip over Kloof Nek road with its spectacular views of the Twelve Apostles, brings you to Camps Bay strip and Clifton beaches.Cape Town International Convention Center (CTICC) the V&A Waterfront, Cape Town city centre and art galleries and museums, are easily accessible.
host_about:
Hello this is Reinhard. I am an infectious diseases physician, a medical microbiologist and a clinical pathologist.
I am a South African and I enjoy living here in Cape Town at the southern tip of Africa.

There is so much to do here: paragliding , walking, running, hiking, wine-tasting and I would like to share gastronomic and culinary delights with you.

Tamboerskloof is a suburb in the city centre at the foot of Lions head and Signal Hill.Fresnaye is a suburb on the Atlantic seaboard close to Seapoint

Deutsch:
Hallo hier spricht der Kapstaedter

Bin ein klinischer Infektiologe,medizinischer Mikrobiologe und auch Laborarzt.

Hier geht es uns gut in dem wunderschoenen Klima Kapstadts.

Zentrumnah gibt es so viel zum unternehmen und ich helfe Euch dabei.

Es gibt hier viele gastronomische Erlebnisse. Fragt mich.

Und auch so vieles zum Sehen hier in der afrikanischen Stadt des Wohlbefindens.

comments:
Nous aurions pu y passer un séjour idyllique. Le logement correspondait à nos attentes, il était confortable, bien équipé et dans un très joli quartier. Le passage quotidien de la femme de ménage était appréciable mais nous aurions préféré que ce ne dure pas toute la journée pour plus d'intimité.
Nous avions été prévenus par Carol que des travaux de construction étaient en cours, en journée, la semaine. Dans les faits, la maison était entourée de 3 chantiers différents (2 en face et 1 dans le terrain attenant). Il y avait un chantier de terrassement où 2 tractopelles et plusieurs camions se relayaient de 8 h à 17 h non stop. Nous n'avons pas pu passer un moment calme en journée au bord de la piscine (sans bruit et sans poussière). Malgré le fait que Carol ait fait un geste commercial au moment de la réservation, nous ne nous attendions pas à tant de nuisances.

name: 3-Bedroom Penthouse with City & Harbour Views
description: Wake up to breathtaking panoramic views of Cape Town in this spacious three-bedroom, three-bathroom air-conditioned penthouse. Floor-to-ceiling windows frame stunning vistas of the city skyline, harbour, and ocean. Perfectly located in the heart of Cape Town, it’s within walking distance of Company’s Garden, Museum Mile, the South African National Gallery, the Planetarium, Long Street’s vibrant nightlife, boutique shopping, and top-rated restaurants.
neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is located on the edge of Cape Town’s Central Business District (CBD), in an area known as the Eastern Precinct. This part of the city has a mix of historic buildings alongside some less attractive 1950s/60s structures. While the area is undergoing urban regeneration, it still has visible signs of homelessness and economic disparity.

Despite this, the location is convenient and well-connected, with 24-hour security patrols by the Cape Town Central City Improvement District (CCID), making it one of the safest parts of the CBD according to the South African Police Service.

Nearby Attractions:

The Company’s Garden is a 2-minute walk and provides a peaceful green space in the city.
The area has several coffee shops and restaurants catering to different budgets, including Truth Coffee, once voted the best coffee shop in the world.

comments:
Typisches Hotelzimmer-like Apartment in unmittelbarer Lage zu den Clifton Stränden. Das Airbnb ist wie beschrieben und vielleicht etwas in die Jahre gekommen. Nett fanden wir, dass es Snacks, Kaffee, und ein paar Drinks bereitlagen. Parken an der Straße war in Ordnung - man findet auch zu den Hauptzeiten in maximal 5 Minuten Laufzeit einen Parkplatz.
Was mir nicht bewusst war ist, dass das Apartment direkt an einer der meistbefahrenen Straßen in der Gegend liegt, und es deshalb tags- sowie nachtsüber extrem laut war. Die Fenster isolieren den Lärm leider auch nicht. Dazu kommt, dass auch die Klimaanlage sehr unangenehm gerochen hat und deshalb nicht zu benutzen war.
Für mich aus den genannten Gründen zu dem Preis nur dann eine Weiterempfehlung, wenn Lärm für dich keine Rolle spielt. Für alle, die einen Mietwagen buchen empfehle ich eine ruhigere Gegend abseits der Hauptstraßen.

name: Your own apartment/ mountain views!
description:
Our stylishly one bedroom home at the foot of Table Mountain has all that guests need to feel comfortable and at home.
Guests can walk into the city or to the bars and restaurants on Kloof Str. Or catch the an Uber or Myciti bus right over to the beach.
neighborhood_overview: The apartment is in the heart of the city bowl in Tamboerskloof. It is less than 2 min walk away from Kloof street, which is home to some of the best restaurants, coffee spots, clothing stores and bar's that Cape Town has to offer. There are also 2 supermarkets along the road, pharmacy, laundromat and petrol station. It is equally as close to Kloof nek which is also home to my personal favourite spot - The Power & the Glory (great coffee & bar at night) , German bakery and Laundromat. If you drive or catch a bus 10 minutes up Kloof nek you will be at the foot of table mountain or Lions head, and if you go a little bit futher over the mountain about 15 min drive you will be at some of the most beautiful beaches in the world - Camps bay and Clifton beach.
host_about: I live between Cape Town & Graaff-Reinet at the moment working and travelling as an Interior Stylist & content creator. I have lived & travelled in many countries and I can not live without music, sunshine, mountains, good food and a good glass of red at the end of the day.
neighbourhood: Gardens, Western Cape, South Africa

comments:
A beautiful location with lots of things to do nearby and well connected to the city center. It feel quiet and comfortable, away from the hustle and bustle of Cape Town.
Walking distance to Kloof St with some great restaurants, cafes and grocery shops. Very well connected on an Uber.
Was checked in by Tom who gave me a mini intro to the place. A short ride to the Lion's Head hike which is absolutely recommended.
The place itself was a great sanctuary with a beautiful view to wake up to. Large and airy. Table mountain can be completely sunny or covered in clouds (aka table cloth). Good sized sofa to lounge and relax. Good bed. All the kitchen gear one could need.
The wifi works fine and I didn't encounter any load shedding issues. There's enough adapters here to connect your european pin devices etc - so don't worry about not getting a ZA adapter.
This is one of the airbnbs I would love to return to in an instant.

name: Camps Bay House with Views, Pool and leafy Garden
description:
With beautiful views, fresh sea and mountain air and a leafy garden, serenity awaits at this spacious Camps Bay house.

Eat breakfast al fresco while enjoying stunning mountain views.

Cool down in the large pool and leafy garden in the afternoons.

Enjoy sun downers on the large veranda overlooking the ocean.

Sleep like a dream in the peace and quiet our street offers as well as the comfortable beds!
neighborhood_overview: Situated on a quiet road in Camps Bay, it's a 3-minute walk to Camps Bay Drive, where you'll find a deli and convenience store. A 15-20 minute walk to Camps Bay beach and a 5-10 minute walk to Beta Beach, a small beach, with huge boulders and a protected bay, ideal for families with children.
host_about: We are from Cape Town, we love going to the local beaches and very good restaurants you find all over town at reasonable prices.  Having sundowners on our veranda watching the beautiful sunset is always a treat.

name: A well located stand alone bedroom
description: An easy and convenient place to hang your hat if you're in the city for a few nights. The room offers privacy of your own place without the hefty price tag :) As the name suggests, this listing is ONLY a room - with an en suite bathroom (shower), a kettle and basic tea/coffee making facilities. The main door access is shared with an office and an apartment upstairs in this trendy Bo Kaap town house. Centrally located close to Bree st, Kloof st etc.
neighborhood_overview: Bo Kaap is a fantastic neighborhood - perfectly central to everywhere whilst still quietly nestled on the side of Signal Hill/Lions Head. It has a great community feel and you will find you will barely ever have to take a taxi unless it's out of the city.
host_about: I was born and raised in Kenya, and now live (most of the year) in a small seaside town about an hour from Cape Town with my partner, two children, Coral and Reef and Chesa our vizsla. I love the outdoors, travelling and all things creative. I am a fashion designer and my sustainably driven clothing label, Asha : Eleven is based between Cape Town, Joburg and Kenya.

comments:
Stayed at this beautiful house for two months and absolutely loved it! The house is very cozy, has a great vibe and everything you need. It’s a very safe, quiet and beautiful area of Capetown with good trails for running and hiking right behind the house. The supermarket and cafes are a 3 minute walk away but other than that a car is recommended to get around. Also the highway is super close but you don’t hear it luckily.
The house has a amazing views over city, mountains and ocean and we loved the terrace to do yoga, have breakfast in the morning or invite friends over for drinks and bbq. The only small downside might be the ground floor bedroom which is a bit dark and does not have AC which is needed on a few of the hotter summer nights.
We also worked from home a lot, there is the office and also another desk in one of the bedrooms, the internet is fast enough for video calls etc and the kitchen is spacious and very well equipped.
The hosts are super friendly and helpful as well!
name: Stunning house in Devil's Peak
description:
This bright, stunning house, located in Devil's Peak, gives you beautiful 360-degree views of Cape Town.

Centrally located, it's the perfect place to explore the city and its surrounds. The house is set up to take full advantage of the magnificent view of the city and Table Mountain.

The house is walking distance from shops and restaurants in a friendly neighbourhood.

The house is very safe and secure with an alarm and an electric fence system.
neighborhood_overview:
From the house you can take walks up the slopes of Devil’s Peak and Table Mountain, which is no more than a 10 min walk. There are a number of parks for children walking distance from the house.

Just up the road, 200 meters, is a mini supermarket (KwikSpar) and a number of cool restaurants and bars, there is even a beautician and hair salon. It is a very vibrant and young happening area.

description: Welcome to your peaceful, light-filled home away from home. Enjoy a private bedroom with ocean and mountain views, Egyptian cotton bedding, and a dedicated workspace. The apartment is calm and secure, with Wi-Fi during load shedding, a shared kitchen, lounge, and bathroom filled with greenery and warm, cosy touches. It's a peaceful and welcoming area, perfect for a leisurely stroll to the beautiful Sea Point promenade, where you can enjoy the serene ocean views. Ollie, my loyal rescue dog, and occasional co-pilot, infuse our space with warmth and enthusiasm. We're thrilled to share our passion about Cape Town, nature and adventure and welcoming you.

I had never stayed at an AirBnb with the host before. Jan was helpful, personable, and a great source of local knowledge. The apartment is very well-located near everything, and also in a quiet setting. The bed is comfortable, the kitchen was excellent, and Ollie (the dog) is a delight.

Jan also encouraged me to confront my fear of heights and go paragliding with him. He did an excellent job of making me feel comfortable, while also making sure to abide by strict safety protocols. I am really glad that I took his advice and flew with him. It was the highlight of my trip.
If you want a quaint apartment that isn’t a party place, where you can safely relax and enjoy this city, then this place is for you. If you keep late hours or want to live a party lifestyle, then this is not the place for you.
At my age, a god night’s sleep and pleasant conditions are perfect, and this place more than delivers on that. I am very pleased with my choice to stay here.
name: Safe and Stunning Views, Perfect Location
description: Welcome to your peaceful, light-filled home away from home. Enjoy a private bedroom with ocean and mountain views, Egyptian cotton bedding, and a dedicated workspace. The apartment is calm and secure, with Wi-Fi during load shedding, a shared kitchen, lounge, and bathroom filled with greenery and warm, cosy touches. It is ideal for respectful guests seeking a quiet and relaxed living space. To maintain a peaceful setting, this space is not suited for guests returning late at night.
neighborhood_overview: Our neighborhood is a secure and safe haven for our guests. It's a peaceful and welcoming area, perfect for a leisurely stroll to the beautiful Sea Point promenade, where you can enjoy the serene ocean views. What's more, our central location ensures that you're within easy reach of the vibrant city center, where you can explore and immerse yourself in the local culture.
host_about: I work as a flight instructor in paragliding and my mission is to share the joy of free flight with people. I'm deeply passionate about nature and always eager to learn and explore. Ollie, my loyal rescue dog, and occasional co-pilot, infuse our space with warmth and enthusiasm. We're thrilled to share our passion about Cape Town, nature and adventure and welcoming you.

name: Peaceful Studio in Camps Bay with Mountain Views
description:
Studio Montana is a spacious, modern, and newly renovated self-catering apartment, located in a quiet cul-de-sac on the sheltered Glenside of Camps Bay.

Enjoy all the comforts of home with full modern amenities, fast Wi-Fi, and a UPS to keep you connected during loadshedding.

The large private balcony offers stunning views of Lion’s Head and glimpses of the sea—an ideal spot for sundowners or a peaceful morning coffee.
neighborhood_overview: Camps Bay is an affluent suburb of Cape Town, South Africa. It is renowned for its white sandy beaches fringed by palm trees and has a trendy nightlife. The studio is situated in a quiet cul-de-sac - friendly neighbours all around you! This house is perfectly positioned to truly enjoy the beautiful sunsets and lifestyle of Camp's Bay.
host_about: I'm a friendly person and very accommodating! I love to travel and understand the importance of a clean beautiful place to come home to after a day of exploring! Should you require anything whilst staying at our place please feel free to ask! Look forward to meeting you! :)

comments:
We had the perfect stay at Tom's Cabin. It is the perfect house for a longer stay with kids. There is enough room, a lot of stuff for the kids to play with (they LOVED the trampoline in the garden), a beautiful view from the terrace and it is only a couple of minutes walk to a very cute beach which is perfect with kids as well.

We really loved the house and felt at home right away. Hopefully we will be back next year!
name: Tom's Cabin
description: Tom's Cabin is a self-catering home within walking distance of the beach, with breathtaking views across False Bay. It has 3 bedrooms, an open-plan living/dining area, 2 bathrooms, a fully-equipped kitchen, a separate workspace with desk and chair, luxury linen, flatscreen TV and free WiFi. It's close to shops, restaurants and other amenities and also within walking distance of the Simon's Town Country Club and golf course.
neighborhood_overview: This part of Simon's Town is very peaceful, making it the perfect spot to relax and wind down while enjoying the beautiful views over False Bay. Being in walking distance of various unspoilt beaches is a bonus and the house is perfectly positioned for avid golfers being so close to the Simon's Town Country Club and Golf Course.
host_about: Tom’s Cabin has been in our family for 34 years and in between guest bookings, we still spend holidays here. We never tire of the beautiful, tranquil surroundings, not to mention the stunning sunrises over False Bay and the beautiful beaches, including Boulders Beach. We joined Airbnb just over 10 years ago and still prepare Tom’s Cabin for guests with the same enthusiasm as for our very first booking to ensure they have a fabulous holiday.

name: Sunset 1008 Blouberg Cape Town
description:
From the moment you enter Sunset 1008 you are amazed and you cannot help but say: WOW!
From the stylishly, modern, colorful interior to the jaw-dropping views of the ocean and Table Mountain (the new Seventh Wonder of the World), it is just breathtaking. Kite surfing haven!
WIFI works during blackouts or load shedding!

Please note: South Africa occasionally has power load shedding which is beyond our control.
neighborhood_overview:
Bloubergstrand and Big Bay are world renowned for kite surfing and wind surfing. The apartment is right smack in the middle of these two beaches. The world renowned King of the Air competition takes place every summer at Blouberg Beach. Definitely something to watch! There are a lot of restaurants in the area for dining out.
The most famous one is Homespun. Please ensure you book in advance - their food is awesome!

There is also a big shopping center nearby, Table Bay Mall for all your shopping needs.
host_about: Our mission is to supply an amazing apartment with incredible views to enable people to escape their normal lives and feel at home. If we cannot answer your question we will find out for you. We are always only a phone call away.

name: Rockpool 1208,Cape Town,mountain and sea views!
description:
Rockpool 1208 is situated on the beach front in Bloubergstrand, Cape Town and has some of the most breathtaking vistas in the world. It has spectacular, full Table Mountain, Robben Island and sea views with world famous kite surfing beaches right on your doorstep. The apartment is equipped with wifi (works during blackouts or load shedding too) and full DSTV.
Please note: South Africa occasionally has power load shedding which is beyond our control.
neighborhood_overview:
Bloubergstrand and Big Bay are the world renowned beaches for kite surfers and every summer the King of the Air Event takes place at Bloubergstrand Kite Beach. During summer season thousands of kite surfers take to the waves and it is amazing to watch from the 12th floor apartment. During whale season (July to Oct) you will be able to see whales breaching along the coast. Dolphins and seals can be seen throughout the year.
There are lots of restaurants in the area - most famous is Homespun by Mat. Please ensure that you book this in advance! Another restaurant to try is De Grendel Restaurant. This one also requires advance bookings. These two restaurants are Master Chef style restaurants and are great! Then there are On the Rocks and Ons Huisie Restaurant walking distance from the apartment.
We also have a new shopping center, Table Bay Mall, 3.2 km away and a 5 min drive from the apartment. Please search Table Bay Mall on internet to view shops.

name: Happy home in Constantia
description: A beautifully sunny home situated in the Constantia Valley in a security complex. The house has two bedrooms both with private bathrooms. A lovely outside patio area. Kitchen with loads of work space. Main room is en-suite and has doors which open out to patio. Second room is very sunny with stunning old copper bed. Easy lockup and go home. Visitors parking is available.
neighborhood_overview:
The Constantia Valley truly is Cape Town’s Vineyard.
From : http://www.constantiavalley.com/

It is a region with a wine making history that dates back to 1685 and which boasts 8 award winning wine estates, Groot Constantia, Steenberg Vineyards, Constantia Uitsig, Klein Constantia, Buitenverwachting, Eagles Nest, Constantia Glen and Silvermist Vineyards. All are within 15 kilometres of the city centre.

Nestled beneath the Table Mountain National Park and commanding vistas of the Constantiaberg and Table Mountain ranges Constantia is the ideal place to base yourself when in Cape Town.

Constantia shares its heritage and landscapes of unsurpassed beauty with the locals who have made it their home. The Constantia Valley receives a steady flow of visitors throughout the year who come to enjoy an idyllic lifestyle conveniently located close to all major attractions.

The Constantia Valley has an appeal that offers something of interest to ever
host_about: Hi my name is Nix, I am a wildlife photographer and naturalist from South Africa. I am married to a wonderful man from Scotland. Together we love to explore this globe writing and photographing it's unique creatures, hopefully contributing to their continued existence. The last few years has seen us living an endless summer as we have been working in the polar regions. My favorite place to be is tracking aardvarks in the Kalahari, red sands and tanzanite skies!!!  Love and adventure are our priorities in life.

name: Pool Studio, with private pool and deck
description:
✨🌿 If you love nature and tranquility, this is the place for you!

The studio is located in Hout Bay, on the road to some of Cape Town’s most beautiful sights. Surrounded by a lush garden on our property, it is fully independent and has direct access to the swimming pool and deck.
👉 access to the studio is via a narrow stone staircase, which may not be suitable for elderly guests or people with reduced mobility.

-king-size bed, bathroom , kitchen, sofa (extra bed)
Suitable for 2–3 guests.
neighborhood_overview: Our house is situated in Hout Bay (20 minutes from the City center) which is a very nice and quite area with full of surprises of all kind and the start of the amazing scenic road "chapman's peak".
host_about:
Leaving France in 2010, for a two years road-trip across Africa, Sandrine's family have decided to drop their suitcases in this beautiful town nine years ago, and Cape Town is a delight that Sandrine loves to share.

But not only Cape Town, should I say, the beautiful surrounding nature of the all western cape.

Our property is located in Hout Bay (25 minutes drive from cape Town)  , 
The amazing scenic road "chapman's peak" starts here. 
Only 15 minutes walking  to Sandy bay, this amazing piece of nature is a  paradise for hiking, riding, sliding on the dune, enjoy the sundowner on the beach!. 
The property is also pretty close to  Llandudno beach (10 min drive) which is one of the most beautiful beach of Cape town. 

Hout Bay is a paradise for all nature lovers, hikers, sportsmen whatever the sport you practice even surf if you don't mind of cold water.

The omnipresent ocean, also, has its share of surprises ;
whales in season, Cape penguins, seals, sharks.... and breathtaking points of view. 

Sandrine is an architect and she can organize for you some  visits of Cape Town , she loves to share the beauty of this city, and creativity , she will bring you for a day in  an artistic journey .
Also for hikers lovers, some beautiful hiking in Hout Bay Can be organised, You just have to discuss  with her.

comments:
Kgomotso’s apartment is your home away from home, and she’s the kindest, nicest and most responsive hostess on Airbnb you’ll ever meet.
We stayed in this place with my wife for over 6 weeks, and loved every single moment.
First of all, the location is fantastic - just off the Main Road, but far enough to not hear the noise. Plenty of great restaurants around, offering all sorts of quick brekkie, lunch, coffee or dinner options. Easily +10 great quality spots all less than 10 mins walk.
Secondly, the apartment itself is in a very secure complex, on the fifth floor, offering great views on the Lions Head, and a massive terrace with great views on Signal Hill. Lots of plants and nice design accents that make you feel someone really lives here and it’s not just another soulless rental place.
Lastly, there’s a great and stable WiFi with 60-70 mb/s connection at all times, even during load shedding. We both worked remotely during the entire stay, and never had any issues with internet.
name: Sea Point Views
description: Spectacular views in glorious Sea Point.  A gem with an artistic flair. Walk to all amenities in the safe, friendly neighborhood.  LGBTI most welcome.  Small dogs only.
neighborhood_overview: Relaxed, friendly and hipster neighbourhood with a combination of young families & pensioners. One block from gorgeous CT promenade. Tons of shops and restaurants within walking distance.  A few steps to public transport. SAFE.  When I get to Main Street, Turn right for 8 restaurants. At Main Rd turn left for the best pizza in CT called El Posticino. Walk a few steps more and u come to a few restaurants there. La Boheme has great tapas. Duchess or Wisbeach is a few doors from the apartment, and they are dog friendly.

comments: When I booked this spot the host immediately messaged me thanking me for the booking and welcoming me. I unfortunately did not have a good stay here however. There were about a dozen issues with the property upon arrival. There was no shower curtains, bathroom essentials were either missing or broken, the house alarm did not work and the bedroom blinds did not cover the windows. There were also a range of amenities simply missing. The windows were covered in a thick layer of dirt and grime and the kitchen chopping boards had mold on them. Then there was another break-in on the property because the alarm did not work. I canceled/left the next morning and was refunded for my entire stay as well as being covered for alternative accommodation. This place has HUGE potential and the host was communicative throughout but the property has unfortunately gone down hill. If all of these issues were resolved (in particular an outside alarm) then I would still recommend this place.
name: Muizenberg, Self Catering Flatlet
description:
This mountain facing 2 sleeper self containing flatlet has 1 huge queen size bed.

It is within 10 minutes walking distance to Muizenberg beach and the train station.
neighborhood_overview:
Muizenberg is situated right on the water’s edge of the warm Indian Ocean, on the north-west corner of False Bay, Cape Town, with miles of white sand beaches popular among bathers, anglers and surfers alike. The train station is situated on the rocks above the beach.

Blue Bird Garage Market takes place every Friday night on the edge of this seaside village. It is filled with a collection of local Artisans, specialty cooks, bakers, producers and Designers. Handpicked South African wine, craft beer, delicious food, live music from local artists is on offer.

Muizenberg is also the perfect place to learn to surf, there are a few surf schools on the beach front.
host_about:
I'm a Cape Town local that splits his time between Sweden and South Africa. My hospitality training comes from my schooling at Cape Town Hotel School. I have worked as a chef, concierge, night audit manager in a 5 star hotel, waiter and barman amongst other things within the industry. I believe I have a balanced understanding of what guests expect and need.

My interests include cargotecture, photography, snowboarding and traveling.
I've setup a company that recycles old shipping containers into low cost homes and various other products

African Travel Desk is my web based company that I started when I was 24 that's financed all of my travels and lifestyle.

Just trying to live the dream everyday!

comments: Das Haus in Vredehoek hat in jeder Hinsicht unsere Erwartungen übertroffen. Das Haus ist mit allem ausgestattet, was das Herz begehrt. Zudem hatte Kate einen kleinen Vorrat an Kaffee, Tee, Gewürzen, Öl u.v.m. bereitgestellt. Der Blick von der Terrasse ist atemberaubend. Man sieht den Signal Hill, Lions Head und den Hafen. Zahlreiche Cafés und ein Spar zum Einkaufen haben die Gegend für uns perfekt gemacht. Kate war eine tolle Gastgeberin, die sich sehr schnell um alles gekümmert hat. Uns als Teetrinker fehlte im Haus eine Teekanne. Kate war so freundlich diese zu kaufen und am nächsten Tag vorbeizubringen. 5 Sterne für Gastfreundschaft und Ausstattung. Wir kommen wieder.
name: Beautiful 3 Bedroom Vredehoek Home
description: A newly renovated three bedroom home situated at the foot of Table Mountain - tastefully furnished with a gorgeous garden & outdoor entertainment area, boasting panoramic views of the Cape Town city bowl, Lions Head and magnificent coastline.
neighborhood_overview:
The house is located in relaxed Vredehoek – a young, peaceful and safe Cape Town neighborhood with a vibrant strip of eclectic, highly-rated restaurants and a well-stocked grocery store only a five minute walk away.

We have some incredible mountain trails right on our doorstep for the hiking and mountain biking enthusiasts.
host_about:
Hi, I'm Kate!

name: Beau Cap House
description:
Large house in central historical Bo-Kaap. All modern amenities. 2 large bedrooms, each with en-suite bathrooms. Suitable for up to 2 couples, or family of 4 with accompanied children 16 years and older. Please note, the extra fees are for cleaning, "concierge service" and Cape Town drought tax - consecutive years of drought has prompted a high municipal water tax.

Please see important note below on WATER USAGE & ELECTRICITY.
neighborhood_overview:
The Bo-Kaap has been featured in photographs and paintings by famous artist the world over. The neighbourhood is traditional Cape Malay culture that is friendly, open and accepting and where mutual respect is paramount. You will find that neighbours are always keen to make new friends.

Many new coffee shops, galleries and restaurants have opened up in recent years in neighbouring areas and streets making the Bo-Kaap the most central residential area in the City of Cape Town.

Access to the City Bowl District (CBD), Table Mountain, beaches and highways out to the suburbs and airport is extremely easy.
host_about:
Hello, I work in the non-profit, non-government sector that takes me to many parts of the world. My house is a sanctuary that gives me peace that I’m happy to share with others. There is an ancient culture that descends from my roots in Greece that compels me to treat and welcome you as one’s own, for there is the belief that one never knows whether the person we welcome to our house is god or human. 

The house is full of treasures that have come my way during my travels, and each one evokes a cherished memory, and you are welcome to let them enchant you and become part of your memorable time in Cape Town.  

For a more insight on me I can mention that I like good friendships and companionship (our only enduring wealth), good coffee, wine and music. I enjoy camping and adventure, photography, art and all things curious and unusual.

name: Dutch Corner
description:
Neat fully equipped self-catering garden flat suitable for LONG TERM stays also.
R530 p.n.
15% off for a week or longer. 40% off for a month or longer. Minimum stay 5 days. The two single beds can be changed into one king size bed.
Very quiet area conveniently situated between Cape Town, Stellenbosch, wine routes, mountains and beaches. With DSTV, Aircon, fast WiFi (Fibre) also during loadshedding. Big power bank available on demand during loadshedding (e.g. for laptops)
neighborhood_overview:
Dutch Corner is situated in a cul de sac in a quiet upmarket area in the Northern suburbs of Cape Town. There is a green area right next to the house from where you have a lovely view on the mountains of Stellenbosch.
Two grocery stores, a pharmacy and a few other shops at walking distance. Two shopping malls at 3 km distance.
host_about: I'm a Dutch citizen and emigrated with my husband and four children to South Africa in 1976. We never looked back. I was a teacher in Holland, a daymother in South Africa, as I love children, and have been teaching Dutch to children and adults. I enjoy the outdoors, wildlife, singing in a choir - and a whole lot more, but these are the top 3. I am retired now and love to host people from all over the world in our beautiful country.

name: Unbeatable views of the city
description: Nestled at the foot of Lion’s Head in the peaceful, leafy suburb of Tamboerskloof, this apartment offers breathtaking, uninterrupted views of Cape Town’s cityscape. Just minutes from the renowned German School, and a stone’s throw from the vibrant energy of Kloof, Bree, and Long Streets—as well as the iconic Table Mountain and Camps Bay—it’s the perfect base for discovering the best of the Mother City and beyond.
neighborhood_overview: The apartment is in the trendy neighbourhood of Tamboerskloof. It's close to interesting cafes and Victorian architecture, which creates the feeling of living like a local. Walk to Kloof Street and have direct access to Lion's Head.

name: Top floor studio close to beachfront
description:
Stylish top-floor studio in Sea Point on a quiet road, with stunning Lion’s Head and some sea views.

Perfect for two, in a quiet road, with a modern open-plan kitchen and sunny balcony, newly renovated finishes, and uncapped Wi-Fi.

Walk to the famous beachfront , cafés, pool, Green Point park, gym and V&A Waterfront. UPS ensures Wi-Fi stays on during electricity outages.
neighborhood_overview:
I absolutely love the area around Sea Point, Green point and Three anchor bay because its beautiful set between the mountain and the sea.

While walking through the area's streets and meeting its people, you will fall in love with its natural beauty and incredible spirit.

My favorite is the beach front which is one minute away, you can see it from the balcony. Everybody mingles there, is cycling, running or just chilling in the grass. You can dip your feet in the sea or just relax on the beach. This vibey area is plastered with shops, restaurants and bars for every taste (and time of day).
host_about:
Originally from Austria I was enchanted by South Africa and it's people and came to live here in 2007.
I love to travel and enjoy the vibrant, eclectic cultural mix in Cape Town. We live in the beautiful Atlantic seaboard area, between the sea and the mountain. 
During my time in South Africa I have learned to love Cape Town every day more. As a designer, I enjoy finding local gems and vintage treasures. 
As a host, I give local tips and share my favorite spots in the city.

While walking through the area's streets and meeting its people, you will fall in love with its natural beauty and incredible spirit.

My favorite is the beach front which is one minute away, you can see it from the balcony. Everybody mingles there, is cycling, running or just chilling in the grass. You can dip your feet in the sea or just relax on the beach. This vibey area is plastered with shops, restaurants and bars for every taste (and time of day).
host_about:
Originally from Austria I was enchanted by South Africa and it's people and came to live here in 2007.
I love to travel and enjoy the vibrant, eclectic cultural mix in Cape Town. We live in the beautiful Atlantic seaboard area, between the sea and the mountain. 
During my time in South Africa I have learned to love Cape Town every day more. As a designer, I enjoy finding local gems and vintage treasures. 
As a host, I give local tips and share my favorite spots in the city.

Beach Paradise Holiday Home is the magnificent beach house surrounded by nature, between the beach and the national reserve, within Klein Slangkop Private Eco Estate. Almost on the beach, the tranquillity of this spacious four bedroomed home will win your heart and soul.
When you drive through the entrance gate to Klein Slangkop, that’s where the visual evidence that you’ve just entered an private estate ends. Instead, it feels like you’ve just arrived inside a beautiful nature reserve
Situated along the pristine Longbeach shore, near Sunset Reef, looking towards the iconic landmarks of Noordhoek Beach and Chapman’s Peak with nature reserve all around.

name: Contemporary Farmhouse with Garden and Pool
description: Lounge by the pool of this farm-style villa surrounded by lush green garden. The homestead offers a number of cosy nooks, including a peaceful backyard patio, a plush living room & bright sun room. neighborhood_overview:

Your location couldn't be better!
host_about:

name: Treetops: Self Catering Suite
description: Welcome to Treetops, a fully self-catering suite located in the heart of Constantia, Cape Town. With its own private entrance and deck, this one bedroom studio apartment is set within a larger property with access to our tropical garden and swimming pool.
neighborhood_overview:
We are located in a very safe, quiet street within 5 minutes walk of Constantia Village which has everything from supermarkets, coffee shops and fine restaurants to designer clothing and jewellery.

The Constantia Valley area lies at the foot of the beautiful Constantiaberg Mountains and contains 5 historic wine farms dating from the 1600s.

In addition to the local area's attractions, we are within a short driving distance of all of Cape Town most famous attractions including Table Mountain, V&A Waterfront, Cape Point, top golf courses, superb beaches, Kirstenbosch Botanical Gardens, Chapman's Peak drive and the scenic Hout Bay.
host_about:
I love welcoming guests to Cape Town and making their stay memorable. I manage all bookings personally to ensure everything goes smoothly.

Most of my spare time is taken up by my 2 gorgeous Jack Russells (Snoopy and Smudge) as well as improving my golf score!

name: Spectacular views from Century City Flat Cape Town
description: Spacious loft apartment with spectacular views over a wetland estuary looking towards Table Mountain. Secure basement parking for a car. Use of a swimming pool in the complex. Within walking distance to Canal Walk shopping centre , Century City Conference Centre and Virgin Active Gym. Just 10 km from V&A Waterfront and Cape Town city centre. It is a short drive to Bloubergstrand beach for kite surfing,walking or swimming. Close to N1 highway for a drive to the nearby Cape Winelands.
neighborhood_overview:
The flat is within walking distance of Canal Walk, one of the largest shopping centers in the Cape. The shopping center is open until 9.00 pm at night.
Covered Parking is provided in the basement.
The complex is close to 'Intaka' Island - a 16 hectare multi purpose nature area that is ideal for bird watching. There are self guided walks in this natural area in the heart of Century City.
The flat is 10 km from the V&A Waterfront and Cape Town city center.
You can catch a bus into Cape Town from where you can join the sight seeing bus trips around the Peninsula and to the main beaches.
Tours can be arranged to the Wine farms in the surrounding area.

Wifi internet access - speed is 30/15Mbps .
host_about:
I enjoy playing tennis, hiking and going to the theatre.
 I am looking forward to hosting guests that can enjoy the many activities and sights to see in Cape Town and the surrounding areas.

name: Misty Mornings
description:
Take in breathtaking natural surroundings from this beautiful contemporary oceanfront home. Wake to the sound of the waves, take long walks on beaches, relax by the pool, or dine on the balcony with stunning sunset view's of the beautiful ocean and the distant mountains.

Seaside living at its natural best in one of the most beautiful places on earth.
Exquisite design and positioned on Kommetjie beach, Misty Mornings offers a pure holiday experience.

Immerse yourself into the awesome beauty that surrounds you from the comfort of this magnificent, very well equipped beach house nestled in a private estate that forms part of the greater Table Mountain Nature Reserve.

Wake up to the sound of whales. Walk on kilometers of unspoiled beaches.

A truly rare holiday experience that places you into a new world, yet still conveniently close to all you need.

name: Cape Town Gem
description: Welcome to your home away from home! This recently renovated and secure apartment offers you a peaceful retreat in the heart of the city. Conveniently located near supermarkets, beaches, and popular tourist attractions, you'll have everything at your fingertips just waiting to be explored.
neighborhood_overview:
The apartment is conveniently close to city attractions but peaceful enough to forget you’re in the city at all!

Shop for groceries at Garden Centre, which is a short walk of 250m away and visit lovely local coffee shops and restaurants close by - your nearest one just 120m away. There is also a stunning Thai spa 2 blocks down.

There are many beautiful walks and hikes to do locally, but please remember you are in the city and stay vigilant; do not walk trails on your own or as a couple. Better yet, join the local neighbourhood group (for free!) and walk with people who are familiar with the routes. Please ask for details if interested.

name: Seabreeze
description:
Sea Breeze is just that kind of place, from the Fresh, Spacious, Comfortable and Relaxed inside the easy flow to the outside puts you on the doorstep of the vastness of the Sky, Mountains and Sea.

Modern luxurious Beach house with shaded patio and decks that accommodate sun beds to relax and unwind on and take in the beautiful Ocean views and sounds of the sea.

Seabreeze is a Tourism Graded property that offers guests that peace of mind when booking and receiving a high standard of holiday Villa with exceptional service from all involved in your booking and during your stay.

Seabreeze embodies the perfect balance of Stylish and Laidback living - glass sliding doors ease open onto Timber decks -

Every room spilling Mountain and Ocean views - A happy mix of the Raw, the Soft and the Luxurious - come and experience it.

Easy Living, Natural Surroundings, Privacy, Wide Open Spaces, Security

The entire property is accessible

name: Watch the Waves from a Bright, Stylish Coastal Studio
description:
Curl up in the snug reading nook before toasting the ocean sunset on the terrace of this high-end seaside hideaway. Take a sunny stroll on the beach and do some whale-watching, then head home to fire up the barbecue for an alfresco meal with a view.

It is a studio apartment with fantastic views of the ocean and lions head. The apartment comfortably accommodates two people and with the sleeper couch, it is ideal for a couple with a child. It is also ideally suited for businessmen who would prefer not to stay in a hotel as it is a quick 10 min ride into the city against traffic.

The apartment is stylishly furnished with high end finishes and all necessary mod-cons including fridge, dishwasher and convection oven. The apartment enters into the kitchen and open plan living room. There is a breakfast nook/reading corner with a mountain view. The kitchen has a large centre island with breakfast seating for two. The lounge has a large, comfortable 2-seater couch wi
neighborhood_overview: Camps Bay is a stunning place, with natural beauty and unique views of both the ocean and the mountains. Walk to Bakoven beach in five minutes, or head across to Camps Bay's popular beaches and restaurants in 20 minutes.
host_about: Bernhard and I live in Camps Bay with our three children.  We love the outdoors, the beach, the mountains and experiencing those things with our children.  We  are grateful to live in one of the most beautiful places in the world and look forward to meeting new people through this.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

Danke für das wunderbare, frisch gebackene Brot und den wunderbaren Wein von Buitenverwachting, Milch und Butter. Fühlte mich willkommen.
name: Watch the Waves from a Bright, Stylish Coastal Studio
description:
Curl up in the snug reading nook before toasting the ocean sunset on the terrace of this high-end seaside hideaway. Take a sunny stroll on the beach and do some whale-watching, then head home to fire up the barbecue for an alfresco meal with a view.

It is a studio apartment with fantastic views of the ocean and lions head. The apartment comfortably accommodates two people and with the sleeper couch, it is ideal for a couple with a child. It is also ideally suited for businessmen who would prefer not to stay in a hotel as it is a quick 10 min ride into the city against traffic.

The apartment is stylishly furnished with high end finishes and all necessary mod-cons including fridge, dishwasher and convection oven. The apartment enters into the kitchen and open plan living room. There is a breakfast nook/reading corner with a mountain view. The kitchen has a large centre island with breakfast seating for two. The lounge has a large, comfortable 2-seater couch wi
neighborhood_overview: Camps Bay is a stunning place, with natural beauty and unique views of both the ocean and the mountains. Walk to Bakoven beach in five minutes, or head across to Camps Bay's popular beaches and restaurants in 20 minutes.
host_about: Bernhard and I live in Camps Bay with our three children.  We love the outdoors, the beach, the mountains and experiencing those things with our children.  We  are grateful to live in one of the most beautiful places in the world and look forward to meeting new people through this.

name: Waterfront Views from Condé Nast Featured Apartment
description:
Barbecue on the balcony while enjoying far-ranging stadium and waterfront views. This renovated property was featured in Condé Nast House & Garden, and with highlights like a marble-finished kitchen and big walk-in rain shower, it's easy to see why.

129 square meters corner apartment with lots of views. One on suite king bedroom with sleeper couch and one twin bedded room makes it 4 beds in total.

Discover a wide variety of dining and shopping options around the corner from this apartment.
neighborhood_overview: Discover a wide variety of dining and shopping options around the corner from this apartment. The city centre and V&A Waterfront are within walking distance as well, while just a short drive will reach the beaches and Table Mountain.
host_about: In 2014, my wife and I started our Airbnb journey with sharing our home in Cape Town with guests from all over the world. It seemed like a strange idea at first, but we loved the experience and learnt so much from it. Today, we own a few properties and guests no longer stay together with us, but we still find hospitality equally important and want our guests get a sense of us when visiting any of our spaces. name: Everview Suite
description:
NO LOAD SHEDDING! (No electricity outages at all!)
Fantastic apartment located in Camps Bay, Cape Town with expansive views of the atlantic ocean and Table Mountain. Walking distance to Camps Bay and 10min drive from the city. 3 beautiful en-suite bedroooms and a comfortable open plan living area.
neighborhood_overview:
Camps Bay is a wonderfully central place to explore Cape Town from (just a 10 min drive into the city) whilst still being out of the hustle and bustle of the city. The Table Mountain Cable Car is also just a 10min drive away and the V&A Waterfront is about a 20min drive away.

It is an amazing place to live with so much natural beauty and unique views of both the ocean and the mountains.
host_about: Bernhard and I live in Camps Bay with our three children.  We love the outdoors, the beach, the mountains and experiencing those things with our children.  We  are grateful to live in one of the most beautiful places in the world and look forward to meeting new people through this.

comments:
Highly recommend this special spot in Camps Bay. We stayed there with our two, 14 year old sons and the three bedrooms worked perfectly for us. The place was clean and the host was really responsive and helpful. We were even met with a fresh loaf of bread on arrival (not sure if that can always happen, but was a lovely special touch.) Cleaning happened daily, too, so that place looked terrific. Indoor/outdoor design is impressive and view of ocean with mountains behind is quite spectacular. The small pool also offered a good dip on the hot days.
Restaurant and grocery is either a five minute walk up the hill or a 10-15 minute walk down, which takes you to the spectacular beach with lots of very good water-front restaurants. If it's warm enough, try going for a swim in the human made ocean-water pool by the beach. It's cold but offers a memorable view of Table Mountain from the ocean.

name: Stylish Apartment with Spacious Balcony
description:
Admire the ships as they come in to Cape Town Harbour from this apartment's balcony overlooking Seapoint Promenade. Soak up the sun and sea air on the spacious terrace and enjoy some al fresco dining with wine poured from the personal bar. In addition, an inverter has been installed should there be load shedding

A modern and warm living area welcomes you with a smart TV and fully equipped modern Kitchen. The apartment has 2 gorgeous bedrooms both with extremely comfortable kingsize beds. Both beds have luxury kingsize pillows with crisp white egyptian cotton linen by Granny Goose. There are 2 full modern bathrooms (one en-suite). A large outdoor balcony with a view of the sea & promenade allows for alfresco dining and loungers to soak up the Cape Town sun. A Barbeque is on the patio for you to enjoy a South African braai!!

The location is just a few steps from the beachfront promenade & MyCiti Cape Town transport system. The MyCiti bus allows you to move a

name: The Art To Live On Top Of The World
description:
Drift off under a star-filled African sky and greet the morning with breathtaking vistas of the entire Hout Bay valley. Nestled in a serene setting, your cozy cottage offers direct access to the Nature Reserve, perfect for nature lovers seeking adventure and tranquility. Embark on picturesque walks on the stunning Llandudno and Sandy Bay beaches, surrounded by the beauty of the Cape.

Come, relax, and recharge in this peaceful haven where nature’s wonders await just outside your doorstep. 🌿🏞️
neighborhood_overview: It's really quiet up here on the mountain, away from the main roads.
host_about: I'm a German who's explored the world, having lived and worked everywhere from Europe and Asia to the Middle East and the US. You'll find me at my beautiful home near Cape Town, which serves as an art gallery and is wonderfully situated right in the heart of a nature reserve. Come discover this special retreat!

description: Enjoy front sea views, comfort, & unbeatable convenience in Cape Town's most popular area. It is also popular because it is full of restaurants, bars, shops and activities spread along its long shoreline.

name: Luxury House-Share in Camps Bay
description:
A wonderful house-share with open-minded, laid-back housemates. Perfectly located, with great sea views and incredible sunsets.

The common area has 200Mbit wifi, Ren the adorable cat , 4K movies on a big TV and electricity 24/7. Spacious lounge, full kitchen, refreshing pool, sound system & perks like laundry and 2x weekly cleaning by our wonderful Shaheida.

Room & house are not suitable to work from home or remotely.
neighborhood_overview:
Camps Bay is probably the most exclusive suburb in the whole of Africa and due to it’s location very safe and surrounded by beautiful mountains and the sea.

While I personally prefer to go out in town, which is 5-10 minutes away via Uber ($5), Camps Bay offers a nice promenade, excellent restaurants & stunning beaches.

Good news is, that there is no way to spoil the fact that you have landed on one of the most beautiful spots of this planet. The view from the house or any walk either on the beach or the mountains behind stay absolutely magical. Also, there are amazing, local places to be discovered within a few minutes drive/taxi.
host_about: Hi there! Living in Berlin and Cape Town, and traveling other places as well, I've been using Airbnb for quite some time and met some cool hosts on the way. You can rent my apartment in Berlin or stay at a room in my house in Camps Bay, Cape Town - and probably even meet me there. :)
neighbourhood: Camps Bay, Westkap, South Africa

comments: Sharons place is modern, clean, and you feel right at home from the time you step in. She was responsive when we had queries, and fixed issues as they arose, although there weren't many and we did stay there for 2 months. The view onto Table bay and Table mountain is incredible, and the privacy of the flat is great. Kitchen had all the amenities we needed were there. The WIFI is fast and stable, and we were able to watch UK TV without it buffering, and use the internet without any issues. With load shedding it's also a really handy thing to have a UPS, which is included,  thus even when you have an area outage you still have wifi. The Location is near all the Bars and restaurants, and you literally overlooking the beach front. All in All, an outstanding place, and outstanding host.

name: CAMPS BAY: Magic & Meters from the  Sea
description:
Fully equipped apartment attached to the oldest property in Camps Bay (see black & white pic from over 120 years ago)
Few meters away from world famous Camps Bay beach.
Very spacious with small kitchenette too which comprises of microwave, fridge, toaster & kettle. WIFI throughout.
Adjacent to newly constructed 5 Star Marriott Hotel.
Pool available to dip into after a great day at the beach!
neighborhood_overview:
Paradise in every sense of the word!
Beach, mountains and awesome vibe!
(I've lived in Camps Bay all my life and even though I travel extensively, cannot imagine anywhere more idyllic to call home!)
host_about:
Kim Faclier is Managing Director, Property: Liquidity Services SA (Pty) Ltd. the African representative of Liquidity Services Inc. listed on NASDAQ (LQDT).
She has enjoyed more than 30 years of enthralling experience in real estate across the commercial, residential, industrial as well as the online auction sectors. Faclier is an evangelist for empowering and trusting others, turning pandemic-driven problems inside out and helping her clients view issues through a different lens. Her savvy networking capabilities bless her with a global family of collaborators, calibrated to act as a powerful collective resource in getting things done and ensuring delivery on both promise and project alike!
Liquidity Services has an intuitive, state-of-the-art marketplace called (Website hidden by Airbnb) that aggregates assets from all of their Liquidity Services related websites.  With more than USD8 billion in completed transactions, AllSurplus has over 3.6 million registered buyers and 14, 000 sellers across the globe.
Faclier capably led the company to the Fast Growth Award from South Africa’s National Business Awards and also boasts a trophy case of national and international entrepreneurship and business leadership awards, all which lay claim to her diverse career.
On any given day, you will find her running along the promenade in Camps Bay or descending Lion’s Head Mountain in Cape Town, organizing the South African travels of friends to ensure best-ever experiences, negotiating fresh auction stock in a property market as “buoyant and bouncy” as the trampoline that occupies her morning coffee breaks.
Passion, purpose and meeting different people from around the world are the nutrients which sustain this business dynamo and propel her every decision as she blazes new paths in every endeavor that commands her attention.
A travel veteran of 70 countries,  her most prized asset are her beloved parents, closest family members, which include her three nephews and niece, and of course, her wide circle of friends, not just in Cape Town, but across the globe!
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

comments: Our delightful stay at an Airbnb hosted by Karin was excellent! We like the personal meet and greet with quick orientation of the house. From the moment we arrived, her responsiveness and helpfulness were evident. Check-in and out process were smooth. The studio apartment, described as spacious and cozy, stood out with its ingenious walk-in robe tucked away in the cellar—a unique feature rarely found elsewhere. Property also have safe and security guard. The marbled bathroom and toilet were luxurious and spacious; complete with towel hydro heating for a luxurious post-shower warmth. The well-equipped kitchen made cooking a breeze with generous refrigerator space.  For us the location is good as we have a car, just a 4-minute drive from the shops and amenities, offered easy parking and breathtaking views of the bay and majestic mountains. In summary, our stay was excellent, leaving us feeling safe, pampered, and grateful for the great experience. 🙌🏼🏡✨

Designer interior and a kitchen with every gadget possible. Bedroom one has a king bed, en-suite shower. Bedroom two has a queen bed. Main bathroom has a bathtub. Features are aircon, TV, Soundbar, egyptian cotton sheets, high-speed internet, printer, off-street parking.
neighborhood_overview: Upmarket suburb, the Sea-Point promenade is popular with locals and tourists jog, cycle or walk their dogs. There are plenty of restaurants and shops and bus routes. The best beaches and the V&A Waterfront and city center are within easy walking distance.

name: The Studio at 33 Strawberry Lane in Leafy Constantia
description:
Great for short business stays. Rare long weekend cancellation from 3-8 February.

Pass through the shaker doors and into a wet-room-style bathroom with local Ecoco toiletries and heated towel rails. There is a ceiling fan for hot days. Then have breakfast on the padded seats in the private courtyard, washed down with some Nespresso topped with frothed milk.
Should you want an early breakfast, I can provide a delicious basket of fresh items in your fridge the night before. Also I can supply a small basket of hot muffins or scones in time for your meal. There will be an extra charge for this depending on what you would like.

THE STUDIO

The studio (which is ideal for a couple or a single person) while small in space is compact( 19 square metres) and immaculately finished White wooden floorboards, high white wooden ceilings and white walls give it a feeling of spaciousness. White Plantation shutters give you privacy and complete the picture. Two comf
neighborhood_overview: Follow mapped hiking trails right from the gate of this property in the verdant Constantia Valley. Mountains and vineyards are all around, with wide-ranging shopping areas just down the road. Take the nearby highway to beaches and Cape Town.
host_about:
After years of catering for small exclusive functions, owning a bistro and giving cookery classes, Jenny Johnson has decided to fulfill a dream of opening up accommodation in a small studio and in the Cottage. She enjoys people and would like to share her lovely location with others. She has a small sourdough bakery. If you staying on a Friday you can buy some of her delicious breads. 
Her cats are treated like children.

name: Cosy room in rustic home (Room2)
description: A bright, airy room in a sunny, secure, centrally-located house in the colourful suburb of Observatory. A 5-minute walk from the world famous Groote Schuur Hospital, 15-minute drive from the airport, 10-minute drive from the city centre and close to public transport.
neighborhood_overview:
Observatory, (affectionately known as "Obs") is one of the most centrally-located and convenient areas to stay if you are new to Cape Town. There are supermarkets, restaurants, bars, junk shops and gyms all within walking distance.

Its also a multicultural hub of diversity which is why I enjoy living here. It has housed many activists and students over the years because of its proximity to the University of Cape Town.

The world-renowned Groote Schuur hospital is just across the main road, (about 5 minute's walk away) and located on the majestic slopes of Table Mountain. The world's first heart transplant took place here in the 1960's.

Observatory attracts a fair amount of beggars to its streets but they are generally friendly and harmless.

comments: We stayed at Cassie's beautiful home for just over two weeks. It easily accommodated our extended family of 6. The home is spacious and located in a great, peaceful location with easy access to the highway - in a few minutes we could be in the Constantia wine region, or the city. Every morning we were woken by birdsong to a beautiful sunrise. The views from the balconies and the two bedrooms upstairs are stunning, looking straight at the mountains. We appreciated that the house is stocked with lots of cutlery and crockery, cooking pans and pots and baking dishes, spices and herbs and we were welcomed with coffee pods, wine, olive oil, fresh water and rusks - very thoughtful after a long trip. We also enjoyed great sleep - the beds are super comfortable and the bedding is pristine. Cassie is the best host - very responsive and helpful any time we needed something (like locate a pharmacy in the evening). Overall, what we loved most, is that we felt at home. Thank you, Cassie!
