comments: the house is such a nice location! we took the train to Simonstown, the car to Kalk bay (trains don't run on Sundays) and walked to the lovely beach.
name: Amalfi Large tranquil home above Fishhoek beach.
description: Amalfi is a lovely family home, built into the quiet mountainside above Fish Hoek's  wonderful child friendly beach. It has spectacular views and a huge veranda for al fresco living. The garden is large and stepped. Perfect for family get togethers. Come and unwind and relax on our wonderful sheltered veranda whilst gazing out over the ocean and up at the mountains. Too good to be true...? Try us and see!
neighborhood_overview: Fish Hoek, is a great village, its a 'real place' and has all the proper shops you might need like three supermarkets a fish shop butchers newsagents and chemists. The beach boasts a wonderful cafe which caters for the usual beachy snacks and ice-creams but also does more formal dining too. There are several very good restaurants in the village and a pub too, so possible to leave the car at home and walk down! Fish Hoek beach is wonderful, sandy safe for kids, patrolled by life guards. It has a  boating club and a Hobby club too. The young of the area train to be junior life guards at the weekends. You can walk from our house down the public steps to the beach or drive down and park right next to the beach for babies. We have two lovely villages either side of us, Simon's Town and Kalk Bay, both very exciting places to explore with great eating places too. We are near to Cape Point which is well worth a visit. We also have a wonderful colony of penguins just past Simon's Town at Boulde
host_about: Gordon and I live in London, he is an architect and I a teacher. Our hope one day is to retire to our beautiful Amalfi ........One day!  We have two grown up sons, one is an artist and his work can be seen in the house. The other a landscaper and his input can be seen in our lovely garden at the house. Amalfi truly is a family home, although we have recently renovated, the house was Gordon's family home and has been in the family for forty years. Because we have friends and family there we truly know the area and are good for advice on most things of local interest. Our sons grew up spending their weekends with their grandparents at the house and so we all have a deep love of the place. We use the house for large family get togethers , Gordon's brother and family live in Australia and we all congregate yearly at Easter time. We boast four generations ranging from two months to 94 years! Amalfi is a house to be enjoyed, although we do look after it carefully it is a house that enjoys the hustle and bustle of family life, we hope its a ' home from home' Hope this gives you a flavour. I have only recently added the listing and so there are more internal photos still to be added.

name: Bon Bini Beach House
description: A great spot, just 280m from Long Beach in Kommetjie with an enclosed private garden and sea views from upstairs... a perfect beach holiday ideal for surfers or families with small children.
neighborhood_overview:
Kommetjie is a very special rustic and friendly village, a rare gem in the modern world. It is situated on the Atlantic Coast of the Cape Peninsula. Kommetjie will quickly creep its way into your heart and once you have spent some time here, it will become a favorite hideaway. A peaceful place, yet full of adventure. From surfing one of South Africa’s top surf breaks, kitesurfing, crayfishing and fishing to beautiful walks along unspoiled beaches, birdwatching, relaxing to the sound of crashing waves or just watching a spectacular sunset, Kommetjie has it all, it is an ideal family holiday.
Only 40 minutes from Cape Town, this village is all about kicking back, family time and hanging out - a surfer's paradise in a trendy seaside village.
host_about: Hi, my name is Frankie and I live in Kommetjie with my husband and two sons - we think this is the best place in the World and feel privileged to raise a family here. Please feel free to contact me should you require any information or would like to discuss any of the houses listed. I hope that you have a wonderful stay in our beautiful village and I am sure I shall see you again and again.

comments:
We had a great stay at William and Nicole's Airbnb! It's in a very nice area, felt very safe, a very short walk to the grocery store, and quite close to Green Point Park and V&A. The wifi worked really well (needed to restart the router when we arrived, but then it was perfect), and the internet still worked during periods of loadshedding, which was nice. The small balcony had a great view of a tree and surrounding gardens which had a great variety of bird species, which is something we enjoyed.
The hosts were very responsive and helpful, gave clear instructions for checkin and checkout, and often responded to messages within minutes. The space was nice, and very functional. A great little spot for the two of us to spend a few days in Cape Town!

name: 2 bed Table Mountain views no loadshedding
description: This modern 2 bed roomed, air conditioned  apartment is on the 12th floor of a sought after luxury apartment block on Long Street. It has stunning mountain views of Table Mountain and Lions Heads. A great location to explore beautiful Cape Town.
neighborhood_overview:
Long Street has many exciting pubs, nightclubs and restaurants serving a wide range of international cuisine to suit all tastes. I will gladly provide you with a list of great restaurants to visit near the apartment and in greater Cape Town. The famous Greenmarket Square (selling African curios and art) and the serene Company Gardens (housing the SA Museum & Planetarium) are around the corner from the apartment. Please see the photos for a visual feel of the area.

The apartment is ideally located with easy access to all the major tourist attractions including Table Mountain (5 mins by car), blue flag beaches like Clifton & Camps Bay (10 mins by car), the V&A waterfront (5 mins by car or 15 min walk), Company Gardens (5 mins walk) & Greenmarket Square (5 min walk).

You can take the MyCiti bus outside the apartment to the V&A waterfront area or Camps Bay & Clifton Beaches. The V&A is great for shopping and houses many restaurants and pubs. The two Oceans Aquarium
host_about: I am a father,husband, entrepreneur & businessman. I am married to Cheryl for 33 years. Cheryl is an interior decorator  We have 3 adult children and 5 grandhildren and a German Shepherd dog. We enjoy traveling both locally &  overseas and love meeting  new people & e experiencing diffetent cultures.

comments:
Perfekte Unterkunft in Kapstadt...... mittendrin und doch sehr ruhig und sehr sicher.
Ausstattung und Blick vom Balkon auf den Tafelberg und den Lions Head einfach traumhaft.
Dank einem zum Apartment zugehörigen Parkplatz im Parkhaus gibt es keine Parkplatz- Suche und das Auto steht sicher und geschützt.
HopOnHopOff- Bus Organisation und Ein-/Aussteigehaltestelle nur zwei Häuser nebenan entfernt.
Wir hatten hier in CapeTown und im Apartment 6 tolle und erlebnisreiche Tag und Brad stand uns via Mail immer mit Rat und Tat zur Seite.
Sollte wir mal wieder nach Kapstadt reisen, dann ist dieses Apartment definitiv unsere Nr.1 und wir können es zu 100% weiterempfehlen!

name: Garden-Cottage & Luxury House Share
description:
Join a wonderful house-share in an amazing space with open-minded, laid-back housemates.

Your room is located a few stairs down the main house. It's quiet & big with your own bathroom, rain shower, skylight window, mini-fridge, safe, fast 50Mbit wifi & a desk.

The common area upstairs in the house has fast wifi, Ren the adorable cat, movies on a big TV & electricity 24/7. Also a spacious lounge, kitchen, pool, sound system & perks like laundry and 2x weekly cleaning by our wonderful Shaheida.
neighborhood_overview:
Camps Bay is probably the most exclusive suburb in the whole of Africa and due to it’s location very safe and surrounded by beautiful mountains and the sea.

While I personally prefer to go out in town, which is 5-10 minutes away via Uber ($5), Camps Bay offers a nice promenade, excellent restaurants & stunning beaches.

Good news is, that there is no way to spoil the fact that you have landed on one of the most beautiful spots of this planet. The view from the house or any walk either on the beach or the mountains behind stay absolutely magical. Also, there are amazing, local places to be discovered within a few minutes drive/taxi.
host_about: Hi there! Living in Berlin and Cape Town, and traveling other places as well, I've been using Airbnb for quite some time and met some cool hosts on the way. You can rent my apartment in Berlin or stay at a room in my house in Camps Bay, Cape Town - and probably even meet me there. :)
neighbourhood: Camps Bay, Westkap, South Africa

comments:
Het is een goede locatie, als je slaapplek in Campsbay zoekt is het helemaal prima.
Alles klopt volgens de beschrijving. Eén opmerking en zeker geen kritiek, Wanneer je van plan bent, veel tijd buitenom en in de accommodatie door te brengen , is er in het tuinhuis geen eigen TV (wel een groot flatscreen met Netflix in de gezamenlijke woonkamer) het tuinhuis terrasje was kleiner dan ik dacht en minder geschikt voor bijv ontbijt. Maar Campsbay heeft genoeg mogelijkheden hiervoor op loop afstand
Wij hadden een paar dagen iets bewolkter weer en als het dan fris is op het strand, miste ik een zonnig plekje uit de wind. En toch een TV op de kamer.

name: Luxury House-Share: Full sea views
description:
A wonderful house-share with open-minded, laid-back housemates. Perfectly located, great sea views and incredible sunsets.

The common area has fast wifi, Ren the adorable cat, movies on a big TV and electricity 24/7. Spacious lounge, kitchen, refreshing pool, sound system & perks like laundry and 2x weekly cleaning by our wonderful Shaheida.

This room is perfect for two friends traveling as well singles/couples that are easy with an (exclusive) bathroom outside. Not suitable to work remotely.
neighborhood_overview:
Camps Bay is probably the most exclusive suburb in the whole of Africa and due to it’s location very safe and surrounded by beautiful mountains and the sea.

While I personally prefer to go out in town, which is 5-10 minutes away via Uber ($5), Camps Bay offers a nice promenade, excellent restaurants & stunning beaches.

Good news is, that there is no way to spoil the fact that you have landed on one of the most beautiful spots of this planet. The view from the house or any walk either on the beach or the mountains behind stay absolutely magical. Also, there are amazing, local places to be discovered within a few minutes drive/taxi.
host_about: Hi there! Living in Berlin and Cape Town, and traveling other places as well, I've been using Airbnb for quite some time and met some cool hosts on the way. You can rent my apartment in Berlin or stay at a room in my house in Camps Bay, Cape Town - and probably even meet me there. :)

name: Sea Point/Fresnaye Charming garden pool studio
description: Welcome to your own private slice of paradise! This lovely garden studio is the perfect retreat, with everything you need for a relaxing and enjoyable stay. As you step inside, you'll be greeted by a spacious and inviting living area, complete with a comfortable queen size bed, bathroom, and kitchenette equipped with a bar fridge, microwave, kettle, toaster, and all the cutlery and crockery you'll need.Plus, you'll have access to free Wi-Fi and a TV with Netflix for all your entertainment needs!
neighborhood_overview:
Located in the magnificent exclusive area of
Sea Point/Fresnaye.
is a quiet, upmarket area being ranked 38th coolest in whole world which is a stone's throw from the glorious Sea Point Promenade, outdoor Pavillion swimming pool, shops and popular restaurants.

Lovely walks around the neighbourhood which is why everyone loves to stay in Fresnaye, (witnessed by the many beautiful apartments being built) a glorious view of Lion's Head and the wonderful sea breeze while you sit in the garden.

Our favourite restaurants include, NV80 (varied menu but we highly recommend the Baby Kingklip fish and Caramelised Pear Salad!), Cattle Baron (steakhouse), La Perla (Italian and a crisp glass of Pinot Grigio while watching the sunset!), Mykynos (Greek), and many many more exciting, delicious places to add to our recommendations.

Amazing places to visit in Cape Town ~ Table Mountain (hike up and take the cable car down), a day break hike up Lion's Head
host_about:
WE love people, laughter, fun, honesty and integrity.  

CLIMBING MOUNTAINS


NOTHING IS IMPOSSIBLE........
 YOU CAN ALWAYS TURN A LEMON INTO A LEMONADE.

As you step inside, you'll be greeted by a spacious and inviting living area, complete with a comfortable queen size bed, bathroom, and kitchenette equipped with a bar fridge, microwave, kettle, toaster, and all the cutlery and crockery you'll need.Plus, you'll have access to free Wi-Fi and a TV with Netflix for all your entertainment needs!
neighborhood_overview:
Located in the magnificent exclusive area of
Sea Point/Fresnaye.
is a quiet, upmarket area being ranked 38th coolest in whole world which is a stone's throw from the glorious Sea Point Promenade, outdoor Pavillion swimming pool, shops and popular restaurants.

Lovely walks around the neighbourhood which is why everyone loves to stay in Fresnaye, (witnessed by the many beautiful apartments being built) a glorious view of Lion's Head and the wonderful sea breeze while you sit in the garden.

Our favourite restaurants include, NV80 (varied menu but we highly recommend the Baby Kingklip fish and Caramelised Pear Salad!), Cattle Baron (steakhouse), La Perla (Italian and a crisp glass of Pinot Grigio while watching the sunset!), Mykynos (Greek), and many many more exciting, delicious places to add to our recommendations.

Amazing places to visit in Cape Town ~ Table Mountain (hike up and take the cable car down), a day break hike up Lion's Head
host_about:
WE love people, laughter, fun, honesty and integrity.  

CLIMBING MOUNTAINS


NOTHING IS IMPOSSIBLE........
 YOU CAN ALWAYS TURN A LEMON INTO A LEMONADE.

name: Ocean Views Apartment
description: A peaceful and beautiful designer apartment  with magnificent sea views over the Atlantic Ocean. See and hear the ocean from your bed! Close to Sea Points wonderful array of top restaurants, beaches, nightlife and many attractions.  The most beautiful sunset view.
neighborhood_overview: A safe, secure area close to the sea and close to many attractions Cape Town has to offer a visitor. Easy access to restaurants, bars and clubs. 5 minutes drive to the city. 10 minutes walk to the beach.  Taxis are readily available. Walking is fine but would recommend taxis late at night.

name: Small and Secure
description: This is a small, fully furnished bachelor unit with kitchenette, a single bed and en-suite shower/toilet and separate entrance. WiFi and Netflix is included. It has a separate entrance but is close to our family home. Centrally located and within walking distance of local transport and Cavendish Square. Would suit a female solo traveller/student.
neighborhood_overview: We are close to Keurboom park which is a great place to run in. Also if you want to go to the movies Cavendish is close-by with its vibrant restaurant scene.
host_about: Mother, wife, entrepreneur, professional tour guide, Wine Tourism Promoter, passionate Capetonian, Global citizen who enjoys good wine, conversation, nature and art.

name: Beautiful Room with En-Suite - own entrance
description:
This room is the perfect quiet hideaway for travelers visiting Cape Town. We have made it as authentic and modern as possible to make you feel right at home in a luxurious environment. There is 24hr Wifi and a desk to work from.

Barkly Road is a very quiet street. We are off the grid with our solar installation and loadshedding as we know it in South Africa won't be an issue for you.

We have provided for single travelers or couples visiting Cape Town. You have your own access.
neighborhood_overview: Lovely quiet street in upper Sea Point with stunning views towards the Ocean. Also great mountain views. Easy access to both Beaches and Mountain. Short walk to great coffee, eateries, restaurants, beaches and the promenade.
host_about: We love to travel and hope you enjoy our quiet space for some R&R during yours.

Everything is great except the painful check-in that forced us to pay an additional 200 Rand for "late" arrival after 5pm, and meet a person in the dark in Cape town to get the keys. I don't understand why there is no lock for self-check-in like it is done in other airbnb
I have never seen a check-in considered late at 5pm and even less requesting additional fee for that. It was a bad surprise and we would have appreciated to be informed before
Otherwise the rest was perfect, nice place, great location.
David
name: One-bedroom in De Waterkant Village
description:
This compact studio is located on street level in the heart of De Waterkant. Discover Vos Lane, a charming cobbled road just off Loader Street.
Inside, the studio, located on street level, comfortably sleeps a maximum of 2 guests in a cozy bedroom. Unwind in this designed space, complete with a well-equipped kitchenette, a modern bathroom with a shower, air conditioning, high-speed internet, and a smart TV. Whether you're here for work or leisure, our studio provides a peaceful retreat.

comments:
Would I come back here?
Definitely!
A few points for the owners to consider:
Dishwashing liquid(to clean up after ourselves), a mop(for spills), fumigation or doom spray(for the 2 roaches I killed).
NB: Don't let the roach deter you from coming to this place. I feel as though they entered while the windows were open etc.
Hessen was helpful in checking us in early even though normal check-in time is at 2pm. I appreciated the effort since he does not stay in the area. I'll definitely be back here.
The points mentioned are meant to better the business, not batter it's reputation.
Bed was readily made, bathroom clean, and I was thoroughly satisfied

name: Camps Bay The View Villa Gdn apt & Pvt Pool
description: A contemporary solar-powered, exclusive garden apt with a private plunge pool and garden in Upper Camps Bay, will blow you away with its awe-inspiring views of Camps Bay Beach, Lions Head and Table Mountain. It occupies the entire garden level of a 2 apt. property. Peaceful and secure with Nature Reserve walks just 2 mins away. V&A w/front- 15 mins drive. 3 en suite bedrooms, cable tv, Netflix and uncapped high-speed fibre Wi-Fi. !  Aircon/heating is available in all bedrooms. No power outages.
neighborhood_overview: In a stunning location in  Upper Camps Bay, between the sea and the mountains, the area offers excellent restaurants on the world-renowned Camps Bay Beach strip. As well as the sensational views, there is a stunning nature reserve, hiking and cycling trails just steps away.
host_about:
Calvin and I moved to Cape Town from Durban 17 years ago and have loved every minute of it. The energy here is electric. There is so much adventure here and so much to see and experience. We have travelled a lot and don't think there is a better place to live. We are fortunate to get guests from all over the world and it is such a privilege to host them in our beautiful property and our incredible city- Cape Town.  We have 2 children who have left the nest and 1 Jack Russell.
Motto. You only get one shot at life, so live it and love it - every day!
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

The Villa, 750m from beach, is a 3 bedroom house in Klein Slangkop Estate. This has private access onto Kommetjie public beach and borders on Table Mountain National Park. Pet friendly. Swimming pool, DSTV satellite, Netflix, linen change service 1/week, mid week clean, full kitchen.
neighborhood_overview:
Kommetjie offers a coastal village atmosphere, with beautiful pristine white sand beaches. World renowned Cape Point, part of Table Mountain National Park, and the Boulders Beach penguin colony are close by. Whale watching in season occurs in the bay and nearby False Bay.
Kommetjie is one of the most popular surfing and kite boarding destinations in the country and also has a popular paragliding site. Hiking and mountain biking trails are close by in Silvermine Nature Reseve.
Bird watching and beautiful hiking or ambling trails abound in the area.
Golf - non members can play at The Clovelly Golf Club and at the Simonstown Country Club. There is a tennis court in Kommetjie available to guests.
Various shops offer fresh and frozen meat, vegetables, fruit, bread and pastries and dry goods.
There are 2 local restaurants, 2 village pubs, an ice cream shop and a coffee shop. Fine dining restaurants are in Noordhoek, Scarborough and Kalk Bay a short drive away. Cape
host_about:
I am a keen paraglider pilot and surfer. I enjoy outdoor pursuits including bird watching ,hiking and cycling.
I love fine wine and dining ..

name: Airlie Cottage near Groot Constantia
description: This charming semi-detached cottage sleeps 2 guests. It features a spacious open-plan lounge, dining area, study, and a well-equipped kitchen. The en-suite double bedroom offers comfort, while a wood-burning Jetmaster fireplace adds warmth. Enjoy a private garden, patio, swimming pool, built-in Jetmaster braai, and outdoor furniture. Includes smart TV, DSTV,  10 solar panels 8kV solar system, gas hob, monitored security, borehole, shared laundry, and secure driveway parking for one small car
neighborhood_overview: The cottage is situated in the heart of the Constantia Valley wine route. It is in walking distance of Groot Constantia. It is close to Steenberg, Constantia Uitsig and Buitenverwachting. Nearby is Eagles Nest, Constantia Glen, Silvermist and Beau Constantia. Tokai forest and Silvermine are close by for swimming in the dam, walking and hiking. It is 2kms  from Constantia Emporium and 3kms from Constantia Village Shopping centre. 500m away is the Mosque and the Kramat as well as many churches of all denominations. It is close to 2 beaches facing either side of the Peninsula. You can watch the sunrise in the morning at False Bay or Muizenberg and set in the evening at Hout Bay or Noordhoek/Kommetjie. Security wise it is the safest suburb in Cape Town. Strawberry Security Ass monitors the property 24 hours a day.
host_about: I would describe myself as fun loving and adventurous. I love nature and the outdoors. I would love to share these aspects with you. Groot Constantia farm is a great spot to unwind. The Grootboskloof River Walk is 200m from my gate.   I love to run/walk on Muizenberg beach and watch the sunrise. St James tidal pool is wonderful. I am a grandmother of 3 and a mother of 2 daughters. I love holidaying in Hermanus and in the Eastern Cape, UK and USA.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

comments: We enjoyed our stay with our host Freda - contact with her and her family was good (they themselves stay above the apartment). The garden and pool was lovely during the warm, sunny days and the luxury of daily housekeeping by lovely Jane. Definitely recommend it!
name: Spacious Garden Apartment with View of Table Mountain
description:
Go for a refreshing dip in a turquoise pool and relax in the garden under lush greenery. The tropical feel continues inside with hanging plants, original art, and casual furniture. This bright, breezy apartment is well ventilated and serviced daily.

The space was purpose designed for Guests with simplicity and comfort in mind. The interior style is unique in that it combines Scandinavian and Dutch design influences with unique South African design products and furniture. The space is uncluttered yet personable and homely with creative interior and design touches throughout. Exceptionally spacious, the apartment is ideal for longer stay guest and people with families or babies. The garden and grounds are at full disposal of guests and the tranquil setting against the backdrop of Table mountain makes this a really beautiful place to stay. The apartment is serviced on a daily basis by a dedicated housekeeper who's responsibility it is to look after our guests.

Guests
neighborhood_overview: Oranjezicht, on the slopes of Table Mountain, is the oldest most historic neighbourhood of Cape Town. It boasts spectacular views of the mountain and ocean. Nature is literally on the doorstep. The apartment is also close to the city centre.
host_about: I am a graphic designer and a mother with a passion for South African Design. I enjoy traveling, both locally and abroad. I am equally happy visiting the museums and galleries in Paris, dining at a fine restaurant, or sailing on a yacht around the Maldives cooking my own fish on the braai. I love reading and watching movies, listening to music. I like to think of my hosting style as "barefoot luxury". Easy and relaxed environment filled with the same small luxuries that I enjoy when traveling, good bed linen, good coffee, some beautiful things to look at and an excellent book to read. My husband and I share a passion for the outdoors, he is a big wave surfer with an intimate knowledge of the South African Coastline, our ocean, and the off the beaten track wild experiences that makes South Africa so unique.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

comments:
Denise and Steven are the perfect hosts. Super friendly, the welcome was so warm and personal, they were always approachable and truly taking care of us.
The location is amazing. The view over Cape Town is breathtaking and you can see the Table Mountain in the opposite direction.
We felt very much like being home and with family!
There might be bigger, more luxurious or cheaper stays in Cape Town but you will nowhere else get this level of hospitality and hardly find such a stunning location. Away from the city buzz but still everything is easy to reach within minutes drive.
name: A'Capensis  spacious garden flat.
description:
This popular and spacious garden apartment on the edge of the Table Mountain World Heritage Site has spectacular views over the city and mountains.
It accommodates 2 to 4 guests comfortably, and is located in a quiet secure area above the city, Table Bay and its harbour, with easy access to the city, beaches, restaurants the Victoria and Alfred Waterfront and even medical facilities.
neighborhood_overview:
Very centrally located, quiet, green and with great views, bordering on the Table Mountain Reserve, we are within walking distance of the Oranjezicht City Farm, the very viby and lively Saturday Morning Market on the lawns of the Premier's residence. Our local Deli is within walking distance or just a few minutes by car, you have a choice of restaurants and cosy pubs. The Waterfront and Beaches are a short car ride away with Golf Courses and the Winelands of Paarl, Franschoek and Stellenbosch about a 45 minutes by car. You will need at least 4 to 5 nights to experience this wonderful Cape Town and it's environs! If shorter you will be disappointed.
Please view this short video by one of our guests featuring the Cape Peninsula, the mountains, the sea and our garden apartment.
(URL HIDDEN)
host_about: Architects both, Steven walks and cycles, both enjoy travel, adventure, good design, food and music. Steven enjoys reading and woodwork. Denise excels in pottery and at cooking. We love interacting with guests from all over the world - (we solve the problems of the world in our discussions!!). Steven is soon to be President of the CT Rotary Club, the 'Mother Club'. We foster the ideal of service to the community and positive growth.

comments: This is a comfortable, well-equipped flat which is very quiet apart from occasional passing traffic in daytime. Camps Bay is a great location in Cape Town, though be aware that the house is on the topmost road on the side of the 12 Apostles mountain range, so in my view it'd be best to have a car - the main part of Camps Bay is only a five minute drive down, and central CT is just over the hill past the road to Table Mountain. Alex and Cindy are exceptional hosts, their approach is just right - they live above the flat they but absolutely respect your privacy, while being very quick to respond to any questions you have; they're also proactive with information about events in the area which can affect road closures etc. Alex was very flexible about our arrival and departure times and gave me a warm welcome and a good familiarisation tour of the property. Five stars all round.

description: Camps Bay Accommodation is a quaint self-catering, NON smoking apartment in Camps Bay. Sleeps up to 6 people, in 2 rooms. Wir sprechen Deutsch.

My son, Alex and I are a fun loving, English and German speaking  duo who love  meeting new people, travelling, entertaining and enjoying  good food and wine.

We are looking forward to meeting you soon.

name: Light and spacious house near the beach.

Power back up for all lights, Wi-Fi, tv, kitchen appliances and plugs.

comments:
Thank you so much Kelly! We had a wonderful 3 weeks at your place.
The house and garden are great for families, our kids (2,5y & 8 months) loved it, especially the trampoline, the swings and the pool.
We were checked in by Annette, a friend of Kellys, as they moved up the Garden Route. Annette was there on time and had some really good local recommendations for us. She showed us the house and how everything works (Alarm, Pool, Kitchen).
We always felt safe and didn’t use the alarm at night. You can easily walk to the beach and the lagoon at daytimes along the main roads.
Kelly was also available as we had some minor questions about the pool and the sunbeds, which she took care of immediately and even ordered new sunbeds for us.
She also had some good recommendations for bloubergstrand and grocery shopping.
We think the house is perfect for a small family like us and was a good location for making daytrips to the cape region, winelands, cpt&beaches (eg waterfront 15min ride)

name: The Pink Apartment | Prime Location
description:
*Featured in Real Simple and Home Magazines*

Back on Airbnb after a 4-month full renovation.

Beautifully & eclectically decorated, spacious & immaculate apartment in one of Cape Town's most sought-after areas. Fully-furnished & secure with all amenities to ensure a comfortable stay.

Sweeping city & waterfront views. Close to major attractions: 2-min walk to Kloof St (popular bars, cafes and restaurants); 5-min drive to Camps Bay & Clifton beaches; 3-min drive to Lions Head & Table Mountain.
neighborhood_overview:
Tamboerskloof is a leafy, beautiful and safe suburb, where you can comfortably walk around. The road below the apartment is a very quiet cobbled street - allowing for an extremely good night's rest!

It is also situated close to all major attractions: a quick 2-minute walk to Kloof Street, a 5-minute drive to Camps Bay & Clifton and a 3-minute drive to Table Mountain and Lion's Head. Literally a stone's throw away from Cape Town's best restaurants, cafes, markets, beaches and bars.

On the corner of the street there is a City of Cape Town bus stop (2-min walk away). Otherwise there are Ubers in and around the area, however, most amenities are in walking distance.

Sweeping city & waterfront views. Close to major attractions: 2-min walk to Kloof St (popular bars, cafes and restaurants); 5-min drive to Camps Bay & Clifton beaches; 3-min drive to Lions Head & Table Mountain.
neighborhood_overview:
Tamboerskloof is a leafy, beautiful and safe suburb, where you can comfortably walk around. The road below the apartment is a very quiet cobbled street - allowing for an extremely good night's rest!

It is also situated close to all major attractions: a quick 2-minute walk to Kloof Street, a 5-minute drive to Camps Bay & Clifton and a 3-minute drive to Table Mountain and Lion's Head. Literally a stone's throw away from Cape Town's best restaurants, cafes, markets, beaches and bars.

On the corner of the street there is a City of Cape Town bus stop (2-min walk away). Otherwise there are Ubers in and around the area, however, most amenities are in walking distance.

name: The Escapade
description: The Escapade is a gorgeous fully restored Victorian home .Entering through  the front gate you will be greeted by a lush garden and large frangipani tree . On the left of the house is a large open plan lounge and cosy fireplace , dining room and kitchen with large oregan doors opening to a back garden with saltwater solar heated pool. On the right  of the house are three spacious bedrooms , and three bathrooms .Main bedroom has its own lounge with large doors opening onto  peaceful back garden .
neighborhood_overview: We are literally a walk across a small park, perfect for children, to a very vibey part of Tamboerskloof.  You will find a selection of hip bars, and  Mexican , Japanese, Portuguese, and Indian restaurants only 5minutes walk away . It is also a minutes drive  from the main road running down into the city or up to Table Mountain and on to the famous beaches of Cape town, literally 8 minutes drive away. However the location of the home set amongst tall trees and greenery makes it an extremely peaceful home with no noise at all from the nearby traffic .
host_about: I am an interior decorator living in a modernised Victorian home with my  husband and two teens .In summer the garden becomes our living room and we sleep with the doors open.   Our space is completely private and peaceful and you will wake to the sounds of birds . We also own a beach house on the West Coast that is avail for rent . We bought it as an escape from the city . It sleeps 12 people if you are looking for a larger West Coast escape .

comments:
I had the pleasure to stay at Rosemary’s place for a month. It was a lovely time and even though I’ve already been in South Africa for 4 month, I felt more at home than anywhere else.
Her dog Heidi is just lovely, she is super friendly but also gives you your space if you are not up for cuddles.
Rosemary is such a kind person and I would always come back to the place. She has so many wonderful recommendations regarding places and restaurants.
Plumstead is a residential area, so there are not a lot of things to do. But you can easily access the main roads or Uber wherever you want. It is convenient when you want to go to the south and north of the city.
I can recommend this Airbnb to everyone. It was a very great time and I wish Rosemary the best!

comments: Die Unterkunft war leicht zu finden. Sie liegt in einer der obersten Straßen von Caps Bay. Sehr ruhige Lage. Das Auto wird außerhalb des Grundstücks, auf einem Grünstreifen vor dem unbebauten Nachbargrundstück geparkt. Von einigen Stellen der Straße hat man einen schönen Blick auf Camps Bay und die weitere Küste. Die Wohnung mit Schlaf-/Wohnzimmer, kleiner Küche und Duschbad hat alles was man braucht. Sie eignet sich besonders gut als Schlafstätte, wenn man viel unternimmt. Die Gastgeber sind freundlich und hilfsbereit. Ein gemütlicher Rottweiler bewacht das Anwesen. Er ist überhaupt nicht aufdringlich und  er bellte auch während meines Aufenthalts nicht. Das Anwesen wird von einem Sicherheitsdienst bewacht.
name: Holiday Accommodation in Camps Bay
description: Our 40m² flatlet offers two self-caterers a spacious bed-sitting room with two single beds, a bathroom with shower, a well-equipped kitchenette including fridge, free WiFi usage and a private patio.
neighborhood_overview:
Our neighbourhood (Kampsbaai/Camps Bay) is regarded as particularly quiet and safe and is only seven kilometres from Cape Town's city centre.
Our guests should definitely experience nature directly behind our house in the Cape Peninsula National Park in addition to the beautiful sunsets and the setting full moon.
host_about:
Ich beschäftige mich vorwiegend mit Sprachen, Gesang, klassischer Musik, Geschichte, Kultur, Wandern, Fauna und der Kapflora. Zusammen mit meiner Frau sprechen wir fünf Sprachen (Afrikaans, Deutsch, Englisch, Niederländisch, Portugiesisch) und auf einfachem Niveau Schwedisch. Wir unternehmen regelmäßige Wanderungen rund um das Tafelbergmassiv. 
Meine aktive Teilnahme am Philharmonia Choir und dem Mountain Club of South Africa (Wanderungen) und diverse Arbeitsprojekte beschäftigen mich seit meiner Pensionierung Ende 2010 als Fachberater für Deutsch im südafrikanischen Bildungswesen. Ich halte gelegentlich Vorträge zur südafrikanischen Geschichte und teile mein Wissen über Land und Leute sehr gerne mit Gästen aus aller Welt.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

name: Tuscan Villa near Cape Town
description: Just a 10 min drive from Bloubergstrand, this villa is ideal for 2 families staying together. It offers fantastic open-plan living and a beautiful garden with a boma-style BBQ area - perfect for long summer days spent around a sparkling blue pool!
neighborhood_overview:
The house is very centrally located between Cape Town CBD and Cape Town's outlying areas so it is an excellent base for seeing it all!

For those who love the beach, famous Blouberg beach with its iconic views of Table Mountain is just a 10 minute drive away.

Cape Town CBD is a 25 minute drive away.

The Cape Winelands is just a 30 - 40 minute drive away.
host_about:
Famille de 4 personnes 10 ans sur Airbnb au Cap en Afrique du Sud, superhost.
Visite familiale

comments: Die Wohnung war schön und ruhig gelegen und entsprach den Fotos zu 100 %. Der Garten war mit dem Blick zum Tafelberg auf der einen Seite und zur Stadt auf der anderen sehr idyllisch. Die Küche ist gut ausgestattet und den Grill im Garten haben wir auch gerne genutzt. Einmal in der Woche wurde die Wohnung von Lameck dem Hausangestellten tip-top gereinigt. Er hat auch gegen ein Entgelt unsere Wäsche gewaschen. Zu Coleen als Gastgeberin können wir nichts sagen, da wir sie bis auf den Empfang bei unserer Ankunft nicht mehr gesehen haben, obwohl sie mit im Haus wohnt. Wir suchen sicher keinen Familienanschluss, aber das war doch sehr unpersönlich. Wir haben fast 4 Wochen dort gewohnt und selbst bei unserer Abreise wurden wir per Wats App aufgefordert, den Schlüssel einfach in der Wohnung zu hinterlassen und die Tür hinter uns zuzuziehen. Die einzigen die uns verabschiedeten waren Lameck und Stella die Haushündin.

Wir verbrachten kürzlich drei Tage in einer Airbnb-Wohnung und genossen jeden Moment. Die Beschreibung traf vollkommen zu: ruhig, gemütlich und ein echtes Zuhause auf Zeit.

Das bequeme Bett versprach erholsame Nächte nach unseren Erkundungstouren. Unsere Airbnb-Erfahrung war toll. Wir empfehlen diese Unterkunft wärmstens weiter. Obwohl unser Aufenthalt kurz war, hinterließ er bleibende Erinnerungen. Wir würden hier wieder buchen! Vielen Dank!

name: Unwind in a Bright, Airy Space with Rustic Accents
description: You'll be cosy at this quiet & secure spot in a perfect Cape Town location on the Atlantic Seaboard. Open the sliding doors to breathe in the ocean air after preparing a meal in the full kitchen. With unique fixtures and an effortless style, this is the ideal Cape Town retreat. Super comfortable, packed with amenities & an off street parking. An Inverter System provides electricity during Load Shedding for lights,  Super Fast Wifi, TV, Fridge & Phone/Computer Charging Sockets.
neighborhood_overview: The apartment is in the middle of Cape Town's action and energy, yet on a quiet street. The main street a few minutes' walk away is filled with restaurants and bars. The Stadium and the city's famous V&A Waterfront are also nearby.
host_about:
My passion is putting together beautiful & functional living spaces. I love the beauty of natural materials and recycling old or antique items.

Other than that cooking, eating and traveling all in a care free manner is what puts a smile on my face ;-)

name: Belmont Cottage - Kalk Bay
description: A beautifully renovated cottage tucked away in the heart of Kalk Bay village. Open plan eat-in kitchen/sitting room with a wood burning fireplace. Main bedroom with en-suite shower room. Five minutes from perfect swimming beach, children's park and restaurants and shops.
neighborhood_overview: Trendy little seaside village half an hour from the centre of Cape Town right on the water's edge. Full guide to the area available in the house.

description:

neighborhood_overview:
Tamboerskloof lies between Gardens and the Cape Peninsula National Park, just outside the reaches of Long Street at the foot of Lion’s Head.

Tamboerskloof is one of the oldest residential suburbs in Cape Town and large, beautiful Victorian-style homes lie side-by-side with art deco in an eclectic mix of architectural styles. Gorgeous views of the bay, Lion’s Head and the mountain make this a popular suburb and an easy one in which to base yourself when in Cape Town.

This lovely suburb, in particular, appears to lie in the arms of Table Mountain. While many of the city bowl’s residential areas recline in enviable positions, up in the foothills of the mountain, Tamboerskloof is at the base of the hill, affected only by the initial incline, although when you’re walking it, it can still feel pretty steep.

Tamboerskloof is perfectly placed to explore the rest of Cape Town and the hum of the city makes its presence felt here. The neighbourhood is quiet howeve
host_about:
Sunny and Stormy Cape Town is where i call home.
Traveled the Globe and now working and living in Cape Town .... Love traveling and enjoy the outdoors I have a passion for mountains nature and the wild places in the world. I like good food good company and interesting people with a passion for life.I know all the amazing places in my home town of Cape Town and use to do guiding.My wife is Italian and also speaks French and Spanish. Come visit us.

host_about:
Kim Faclier is Managing Director, Property: Liquidity Services SA (Pty) Ltd. the African representative of Liquidity Services Inc. listed on NASDAQ (LQDT).
She has enjoyed more than 30 years of enthralling experience in real estate across the commercial, residential, industrial as well as the online auction sectors. Faclier is an evangelist for empowering and trusting others, turning pandemic-driven problems inside out and helping her clients view issues through a different lens. Her savvy networking capabilities bless her with a global family of collaborators, calibrated to act as a powerful collective resource in getting things done and ensuring delivery on both promise and project alike!
Liquidity Services has an intuitive, state-of-the-art marketplace called (Website hidden by Airbnb) that aggregates assets from all of their Liquidity Services related websites.  With more than USD8 billion in completed transactions, AllSurplus has over 3.6 million registered buyers and 14, 000 sellers across the globe.
Faclier capably led the company to the Fast Growth Award from South Africa’s National Business Awards and also boasts a trophy case of national and international entrepreneurship and business leadership awards, all which lay claim to her diverse career.
On any given day, you will find her running along the promenade in Camps Bay or descending Lion’s Head Mountain in Cape Town, organizing the South African travels of friends to ensure best-ever experiences, negotiating fresh auction stock in a property market as “buoyant and bouncy” as the trampoline that occupies her morning coffee breaks.
Passion, purpose and meeting different people from around the world are the nutrients which sustain this business dynamo and propel her every decision as she blazes new paths in every endeavor that commands her attention.
A travel veteran of 70 countries,  her most prized asset are her beloved parents, closest family members, which include her three nephews and niece, and of course, her wide circle of friends, not just in Cape Town, but across the globe!

neighborhood_overview:
In our neighborhood North of Cape Town, the City and Table Mountain, further afield towards the South & Cape Point, the Wine lands or West Coast discover an abundance of diverse attractions and activities.

Mountains to climb, abseil, skydive off. Trails to hike in every direction.

Beaches & shores to sunbathe, view whales, get up close with sharks, meet penguins, fish, and surf, windsurf, sail, or stroll on the promenade. An experience never forgotten.

comments: My husband and I booked Thilo’s apartment for a month, allowing us to explore Cape Town and also use it as a home base for other travels. Without knowing CT, we couldn’t have asked for a better location. We loved feeling like part of a community. We walked everywhere (safely) and enjoyed local restaurants, coffee shops, retailers and even the barber! The Spar grocery store had everything we needed and was a 5-minute walk away. The local square/market is called Cape Quarter and it was amazing. You’ll also see lots of colourful homes, as seen in popular photos. We could also walk to the V&A Harbourfront, the stadium and park, Bo-Kaap and the Zeit Museum. Camps Bay, Lion’s Head and Table Mountain are about a $6-$8 cab ride away. It’s a remarkable location. The apartment itself was exactly as shown in the photos. It’s well appointed, looks freshly renovated and has all the comforts of home. While we were out and about most of the time, it was nice to come home to our own space. Enjoy!
name: Waterkant Views - Stylish House with Jacuzzi and Roof Deck
description: Waterkant Views provides modern comfort and style in a traditional setting. This house  suits the young at heart who love city living and yet enjoy the privacy of a hideaway retreat. Enjoy beautiful 360-degree views of the harbour and the city while sitting in an enormous Jacuzzi with a glass of champagne and a satisfied smile.
neighborhood_overview: De Waterkant is the perfect area to truly breeze the vibe of Cape Town. It's often called "The Village", and that actually describes the are very well. It's the only area in town where you basically can do the essential things all by foot.
host_about: I‘m Thilo from Hamburg, partly also living in Cape Town, Mallorca and The Gambia. It‘s my greatest pleasure to create living environments that make you feel at home. With all the amenities we enjoy nowadays, but firmly rooted in local cultures.

neighborhood_overview: Tamboerskloof was originally part of a farm and lies at the foot of Lion's Head. comments:
Literally everything you could want from an Airbnb. Not only was the home stunning in a fantastic location, but Annick was always super responsive (which was really valued from us given we have two young children) and she was always a pleasure to deal with, .
The views from the house of table mountain and the beach are stunning and we always felt very safe. Not only is the house very secure, but the property manager and housemaid were super friendly and helpful, and it was great being a 3 mins walk away from all the great restaurants in Camps Bay, yet still feeling quiet and peaceful. The whole neighbourhood felt really safe, and we walked home in the evenings without any trouble. It was also super useful being able to have a garage to park our car in.
We had a fantastic holiday, and would fully recommend Annick's place to anyone. The house is extremely well located for driving to other points of interest, and we really couldn't have asked for more. Thank you Annick!

description:

Elegant , stylish , spacious
57 sq m internal 35 sq m patio.
views of the sea and table mountain .

Bedroom en suite .

quiet suburb

neighborhood_overview:
The house is 100 metres , exactly 3 mins walk away from the beach, the restaurants, the supermarkets , the bars and the coffee shops .
It is close to two beaches .. the world famous blue flag beach called Camos Bay Beach and the Glen Beach really favored by surfers .

name: Fish Hoek Risi Road Great Views en suite room
description:
Own entrance to a lovely decorated and peaceful queen bedded room with full en - suite bathroom. Own secluded outdoor area with panoramic mountain views. Secure off street parking.
We have free Wi-Fi

Fish Hoek is the gateway to the Cape Peninsula and only a 45 minutes drive to Cape Town and V&A Waterfront.
neighborhood_overview:
The Fish Hoek area is on the famous Cape Peninsula with its astounding floral diversity and glorious landscapes. Kalk Bay with its artistic shops is close by as are Simon's Town with the famous Penguin colony and Muizenburg which offers surfing for beginners and experts alike.
Chapman's Peak, famous for it's spectacular mountain road, can be seen from your "terrace". The rural area of Noordhoek, well known for horse riding and the Noordhoek Farm Village with it's interesting shops and newly "revamped" restaurants is a ten minute drive away.
Follow the coastal road to Cape Point and The Cape of Good Hope.
Do two oceans meet at Cape Point? You will have to come to find out!
Fish Hoek Park Run can be seen from our house and Zandvlei Park Run is a fifteen minute drive away.
host_about:
Myself and my wife Bev, are in the travel business so are lucky enough  to have travelled extensively. We enjoy hosting people from all over the world and South Africa.
We enjoy sharing our knowledge of the Cape Town area including  Cape Peninsula & Winelands.
We love hiking in the beautiful areas close by including Silvermine and The Constantia Valley.
There are many restaurants in  Fish Hoek, Kalk Bay, Muizenburg  Noordhoek and Simon's Town.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

name: Kaia at Outerkom
description:
Kaia is 50 meters from the ocean and is within walking distance to shops and restaurants.
There is a main bedroom en suite with deck and views of surrounding mountains, a fully equipped open plan kitchenette and dining area. Linen, towels, tea and coffee are provided. Kaia is solar powered.
Kaia is located in the coastal village of Kommetjie on the Cape Peninsula, 45 mins from the city centre, the Waterfront and the winelands and 25 mins from Cape Point.
neighborhood_overview: Kaia is situated in a quiet, friendly neighbourhood, where residents are strongly influenced by the ocean's changing tides and moods. There is a wide range of activities on offer and a number of restaurants and pubs within walking distance of the unit. There are plenty of arts and crafts outlets to visit in the vicinity without the traffic and congestion found in suburbs closer to the city.
host_about: Surfer, diver, traveller, author. Living in one of the most beautiful places in the world with my artist wife and special dog. We look forward to welcoming you to share our small carbon footprint and relaxed lifestyle. Kaia is an off the grid establishment with solar-heated water and solar generated electricity 24/7

comments: I stayed at Bayvista for 3 months and it was the perfect small appartment for my needs. The location is very safe in a quiet neighborhood. The views of the coastline are wonderful and you can hear the waves break on the beach below. Rob's indigenous garden is a bird paradise and I loved sitting out drinking my morning tea watching the natural world go by. Rob and Louise are very friendly and are there when you have questions or need help. Otherwise, they will leave you be to enjoy the setting. The appartment is cozy and has everything you need. If you don't have a car you will need to use Ubers for some journeys as the distance to other areas will take time on foot. In short, this was a wonderful 3 months in a hidden gem of the cape town region!
name: Bay Vista Garden Flat, in Glencairn, Simon's Town
description:
We keep the lights on during load-shedding!

The secluded nature of Bay Vista with no shared facilities, makes it the perfect getaway. We have wonderful ocean, valley and mountain views. The apartment is below the main residence, and offers a comfortable, sunny, spacious self catering home-from-home. Ideal for a couple; can also accommodate a family with a child, depending on your requirements. The indigenous garden with koi pond and stream is lovely for relaxing in.
neighborhood_overview:
What makes us special? The views, the sense of space, the ocean nearby - you can hear the waves breaking on the beach, especially at night. Glencairn beach is down the road (and visible from the flat). And you can see the Boulders penguin beach from the flat, across the bay.
A beautiful hiking trail and nature area start 50 metres from the door.
Visit the penguin colony at Boulders, and the spectacular Cape Point nature reserve. World class free diving opportunities in the nearby kelp forest ecosystems.
There are plenty of great restaurants close by.
host_about:
Quiet, friendly, a retired marine biologist by trade, and photographer by hobby.  Happy to discuss the best photo spots around Cape Town.
Look for robtarrphoto on F*B  :-)

name: Sunflower Garden Oasis
description: Our cozy cottage is a peaceful haven with separate entrance.  It is surrounded by trees.  The ocean lulls you to sleep at night.  It's a 5 to 10 minute walk to the beach, train station,  music cafe's and food markets in our creative village community.  This is an ideal retreat if you are looking for a place to work (we have fibre internet and a back up system for keeping it on during loadshedding) or just be still and rest, away from the crowds, but within easy walking distance from company.
neighborhood_overview: We live in a mixed race community, which is something I love about Muizenberg.  There are a lot of artists, film makers, writers and "Muizicians(sp) living here.  On Friday evenings the community get to meet and gather at the Bluebird Market, an easy walk from our house.  The market is host to food sellers offering an international array of cuisine.  It is a meeting place for social and cultural exchange.  Muizenberg beach is one of the the best places in the world to learn to surf.  There are a choice of surf schools for learning anything from Kite surfing to Standing Paddle Boarding as well as straight stand up surfing.   For those who prefer to dive deeper, there is a a diving school in Simonstown and mountains to climb for those who prefer a view from above.  We also have amazing birdlife at the estuary right across the road from us and excellent yoga and quigong instructors in Muizenberg, who we will happily lead you to on request.
host_about:
James is a musician and music facilitator.  Helena is writer, artist and herbalist.   Our children are passionate about music . Our border collies are bright and beautiful beings who take us for regular walks on the vlei, (or wetland) the beach, the forests and the mountains and sometimes just to the cafe. 
We enjoy the journey of connectivity created by Airbnb - meeting new people from all over the world.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa

comments: This home is situated in one of the best locations to stay in Cape Town surrounded by cafes and restaurants. There's tons of security walking around so feel super safe. The house itself is really cosy with everything you need. Very clean. We stayed there for a few days and the host sent over a cleaner free of charge mid way through our stay to refresh the house. name: Flamingo View
description:
If you love peace and quiet, fabulous views and bird watching this is your place!
This quaint pet friendly upstairs apartment (entrance through garage) offers you a cosy queen size bed, fully equipped kitchenette, bathroom with walk in shower, walk in cupboard, seating area and a balcony offering you stunning views.
Smoking is allowed outside, pool to be shared with owner, private small bbq on balcony.
Scooter rental on demand, transfers optional.
neighborhood_overview:
Imhoff’s Gift is a priceless little crime free gem.
This is the kind of neighbourhood where people leave their doors open, houses have no alarms in general, bikes are just parked wherever, without a lock.
We have access control and cameras on the entrances to the walking path by the lake
host_about:
a globetrotter who after living in 17 countries on 4 continents settled down in South Africa, you'll understand -or already know- why, this place is magical!
My love story with South Africa has been ongoing since 2004, and I will be thrilled to assist you to have the.best.holidays in this gorgeous little piece of paradise. 
Feel welcome, and I will do my best for the flamingos to show up :)

name: Minimalist villa with stunning sunsets & views
description:
Llandudno is a unique place, no shops, no bars, no restaurants. Our house fits perfectly to this zen, less is more ambiance: space, light, stunning sunsets and ocean views. The Japanese garden and terrace next to the solar-heated pool oversee the ocean, and in winter whales can be spotted.
You will love the villa if you like to hang around, do yoga, sunbath, chill, ... but also as starting point for cycling and hiking tours or visits to Cape Town and the closeby Wine Region.
neighborhood_overview: One of the most quiet residential areas in the region, Llandudno lies between Cape Town and Houtbay on the way to the Chapmans Peak Drive. It's a 15-minute walk from two gorgeous beaches: Sandy Bay and Lllandudno Beach.
host_about:
I'm a fan of Africa, I love great spaces, oceans, deserts, tropical forest, birds and wild animals; people, history and different cultures. I love hiking, cycling, game watching, South-Africa is IT. 
When my husband and I first came to Llandudno, it was love at first sight. To us it is the ideal mix of being close to nature, and still having access to urban life, a great place to spend days in a row, but also a 'hub' to travel to Namibia, the Karoo, the Garden Route, .... We bought our house in 2014, and love it when guests from all over the globe come to spend some time in gorgeous Lllandudno.

name: Stylish Bantry Bay Apartment
description: Stylish open plan, self catering  apartment in Bantry Bay, one of Cape Town's best known and wind free suburbs  . Fully equipped kitchenette and  large modern bathroom. Very quiet yet within  walking distance to lively Sea Point Promenade, with many restaurants and lively shops . Also easy access to famous Clifton  beaches, Lions Head etc.   My Citi bus transport system close by so easy commute to City Centre.
neighborhood_overview: Bantry Bay is mostly windfree and is considered one of the best suburbs in Cape Town, yet it is very close to Clifton and Sea Point with it famous promenade  many shops, restaurants, churches, synagogues, putt putt , large sea water swimming pool , Mojo Market , gym  + yoga outlets etc etc
host_about: I am a dynamic, fun loving, granny, mom and business executive. I love meeting new people and exchanging ideas and philosophies. I am passionate about travelling and hiking, and enjoy music, art, theatre,  as well as good food and wine. I have two special children and 4 gorgeous grandchildren and 2 cute dogs. I have an MBA and have worked for a French parastatal - Promosalons- for many years -  I have also worked for SA govt in tourism and helped set up a tourism marketing course at UCT .  I also recently qualified as a tourist guide so can assist with many insights to help make your stay a success and can offer special luxury guided tours. I  am also  an internationally  qualified life  coach and founded an NGO ( Umthombo Wesizwe - to help develop leadership skills for young people living in a culturally diverse society) . I have my own tourism consulting company and together with my late husband have been involved in private airport ownership, management and development.

name: A Seaside Place @ Long Beach
description:
Kommetjie is the perfect place to relax, unwind and find your soul. We are perfectly located, almost on famous Long Beach and close to the village with its restaurants and coffee shops. Arum Avenue is peaceful and safe, in an upmarket area, and we are very affordably priced. Drive through Nature Reserves en route to beautiful Scarborough, Cape Point and Simonstown's Boulders Beach - penguins.
Uber drivers, affordable and easily assessable.
Many shops for groceries and a laundromat close by.
neighborhood_overview: We absolutely love living here, 1 minute walk away from mind blowing beaches. Chapmans Peak Drive and Table Mountain National park to gaze at, while sunbathing, surfing or going for long walks on the beach or the surrounding unspoiled natural countryside. Central and with a friendly village atmosphere, quaint shops, 3 excellent coffee shops, good restaurants. Abundant bird life. Near to beautiful Scarborough, Simonstown, and the famous Cape Point.
host_about: I love living here in quiet yet dynamic Kommetjie. -  the sheer beauty of the majestic mountains and the beaches, fresh air, long walks and the friendly village like community.  There is so much to do here, yet you can just relax and enjoy soaking up the sun if you choose; amazing beaches very nearby as well as wonderful coffee shops, restaurants and deli. It has been said that Kommetjie is one of the most beautiful places in the Cape, and I fully agree.  Watch the birds fly overhead in formation each sunrise and sunset, stunning sight!  I am very into natural healthy living, and would love all my guests to experience the uniqueness of magical Kommetjie! You just may never want to leave.
neighbourhood: Cape Town, Western Cape, South Africa
